speaker: EARTH BOAT ANNOUNCER
text: Please keep all limbs and branches inside. Next, a submarine rises from the water. The front pops open and water gushes out, into a puddle that is littered with hats and suitcases. After a beat, Water Elements emerge from the puddle and gather their stuff. Two WATER PASSENGERS trade briefcases.

speaker: WATER PASSENGER 2
text: Ohp! I believe this is yours.

speaker: WATER PASSENGER 1
text: Oh, thanks! Bernie and Cinder try to take it all in. They look up to see a blimp land on a tall perch. A hatch opens and cloud-like Air Elements float out. As the passengers exit, the blimp deflates and lowers to the perch below, where more Air passengers float on board, causing it it puff back up. Bernie and Cinder join the crowd of Elements that walk into the immigration hall. They are the only Fire figures in the entire scene.

speaker: WOOD IMMIGRATION OFFICIAL
text: Next! It’s their turn for processing. They hurry over to a welcoming WOOD IMMIGRATION OFFICIAL

speaker: WOOD IMMIGRATION OFFICIAL
text: Your names?

speaker: BERNIE
text: Útrí dàr ì Bùrdì

speaker: CINDER
text: Fâsh ì Síddèr

speaker: WOOD IMMIGRATION OFFICIAL
text: Great! And how do we spell that? Bernie, eager, makes a series of fire sounds. The official’s smile drops a little, then bright:

speaker: WOOD IMMIGRATION OFFICIAL
text: How ‘bout we just go with Bernie and Cinder! The official presses his nose onto an ink pad and then onto a document on the table, leaving an official stamp.

speaker: WOOD IMMIGRATION OFFICIAL
text: Welcome to Element City! BIG DOORS OPEN revealing the bustling, busy city outside.

speaker: HOT LOG VENDOR
text: Hot logs! Hot logs for sale! It’s like Manhattan at rush hour, crowded with various

speaker: AIR ELEMENT
text: Hey! Watch it “Sparky!” They spot an ELEVATED TRAIN as it zooms by, sending water cascading over the sides of the tracks.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Hmmmm. Water.

speaker: EARTH LANDLORD
text: Dry leaves. Then slams the door in their faces. At another building, Cinder pushes a doorbell. It catches

speaker: BERNIE
text: Khû kò shá sh!

speaker: BERNIE
text: Rì báì khûr! Utriksh! T’áù ss ksòrìf! Cinder, more skeptical, stands just inside the doorway. Water drips from A PIPE and almost hits her. Bernie bounds to her side.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ha ha! He gestures excitedly, laying out his plan.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ksôdàr ì Bùrdì! He continues running around.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ha ha! SNAP his feet burn through a floorboard. He falls out of frame into the basement.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ah!!

speaker: BERNIE
text: I’m ok!

speaker: CINDER
text: Íkì ss ûr.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Bê ss ksòrìf. Bernie reaches into the lantern and scoops some of the Blue Flame into his hands and pours it over baby Ember’s head. A blessing.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Oh. Delighted and proud, Bernie holds her up and smiles.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Welcome, my Ember, to your new life. Baby Ember takes it in. She may not know what her father is saying but she can feel the love and excitement.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Our Blue Flame hold all our traditions and give us strength to burn bright. Ember climbs up to the cauldron’s edge. She watches in awe as the flame grows. It shoots up and she’s knocked back! Ember takes in the brightness of the flame. Bernie steps in front of the cauldron.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Do I burn as bright? Goofing around, he flexes.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Hnnng. ERgggg. Hmmmm! -- The shop is mid-construction. Bernie pushes the BLUE FLAME CAULDRON into place as Ember, now big enough to stand on it, cheers him on. Cinder lovingly watches them as she hangs a curtain.

speaker: LITTLE KID EMBER
text: One, two! One, two! One, two! -- Bernie and Ember stand in front of an oscillating fan, taking turns making goofy faces and sounds.

speaker: LITTLE KID EMBER
text: Aaaaaahhhhhh.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Aaaaaahhhhhh.

speaker: LITTLE KID EMBER
text: Aaaaaahhhhhh. -- Bernie and Ember work on the shop sign together. Bernie writes “FIREPLACE” and Ember burns in a small flame design. TIME JUMP, the SIGN hangs over the front door of the shop. Bernie climbs down the ladder and joins Ember. They admire the sign together.

speaker: BERNIE
text: This shop is dream of our family! And someday it’ll all be yours Hers?? Ember’s eyes grow in excitement. From that moment on,

speaker: BERNIE
text: Welcome!

speaker: CINDER
text: Welcome!

speaker: BERNIE
text: Everything here authentic.

speaker: FIRST CUSTOMER
text: Then I’ve got to try the kol-nuts.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Kol-nuts coming up! Ember sits on the counter next to Bernie. Imitating him, she taps on her own homemade toy register.

speaker: LITTLE KID EMBER
text: Kol-nut coming up!

speaker: BERNIE
text: Good daughter. Together, Ember and Bernie prepare the order, squeezing logs in their palms to make bite size pieces of coal. Ember hands them to the customer.

speaker: LITTLE KID EMBER
text: Some day this shop will all be mine! Bernie tousles her flame.

speaker: BERNIE
text: When you are ready.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Delivery!

speaker: BIG KID EMBER
text: Delivery!

speaker: FIRE KID 1
text: Two sugar pops please! The bigger kid places a few coins on the counter in front of Bernie and Ember.

speaker: BIG KID EMBER
text: I got it, Àshfá! Ember grabs a sugar pop and sculpts it. She molds the pop to look exactly like the kid. She hands it to them and their eyes grow in AWE. The littler kid takes a chomp.

speaker: FIRE KID 1
text: Hey! Just then, across the store, TWO WATER TEENAGERS enter. They laugh and jokingly push each other, splashing water and knocking things over. Bernie gives Ember a nod.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Water. Keep an eye on them. Ember SALUTES and slinks off. In an aisle, the snickering Water Teens take turns pouring water onto flaming souvenirs, putting them out.

speaker: WATER TEENAGER 1
text: Oops!

speaker: WATER TEENAGER 2
text: Oops! A roiling Ember jumps in front of them.

speaker: BIG KID EMBER
text: You splash it you buy it! Ember’s eye’s narrow, then she suddenly FLARES big!The Water Teenagers boil. A souvenir torch POPs from one of their hands and Ember catches it. The punks run sloppily out.

speaker: BERNIE
text: You show them, huh? Nobody waters down Fire! Ember shakes her fist.

speaker: BIG KID EMBER
text: Yeah! Sháshà r íshà! Bernie, startled, can’t help but laugh. Ember looks up at him.

speaker: BIG KID EMBER
text: Can the shop be mine now? Bernie hugs her and tousles her flame.

speaker: BERNIE
text: When you are ready.

speaker: SOAP OPERA WOMAN
text: The truth is... Two shop regulars, FLARRY and FLARRIETTA, watch the show while Cinder stacks “Kiss Me I’m Firish” shirts:

speaker: CINDER
text: She’s not in love with him.

speaker: SOAP OPERA WOMAN
text: ...I’m NOT in love with you!

speaker: CINDER
text: Ha! Knew it! At the counter, TEENAGE Ember is next to Bernie making coal nuts. She has surpassed him in speed, as he’s slowed down. A CUSTOMER approaches the register.

speaker: TEENAGE EMBER
text: Àshfá, customer.

speaker: BERNIE
text: How about... You take it today. Ember’s eyes grow. This is a big deal.

speaker: TEENAGE EMBER
text: For real? She beams. Words she’s waited to hear since she was a kid. She confidently walks to the register with a smile. Bernie backs away, thumbs up. Ember takes a deep breath.

speaker: TEENAGE EMBER
text: Ráì khìf! How can I help you? A customer with GLASSES places a METAL BASKET filled with

speaker: SPARKLER CUSTOMER
text: All this, and the-- oh! The sparklers are ‘buy one get one free?’

speaker: TEENAGE EMBER
text: That’s right!

speaker: SPARKLER CUSTOMER
text: Great! I’ll just take the free one. The customer grabs a sparkler and lights it.

speaker: TEENAGE EMBER
text: Oh, no, see, you need to buy one to get one free. Ember nicely takes the sparkler from the customer and blows it out.

speaker: SPARKLER CUSTOMER
text: But I just want the free one. The customer grabs another sparkler from the bucket.

speaker: TEENAGE EMBER
text: Sorry that’s not how this works. Ember takes that sparkler from the customer a bit more emphatically.

speaker: SPARKLER CUSTOMER
text: But the customer is always right.

speaker: TEENAGE EMBER
text: Not in this case... A beat, then in QUICK succession, the customer grabs another, Ember grabs it, they go back and forth, as Ember’s frustration builds and she gathers a BOUQUET of sprinklers in her hand:

speaker: TEENAGE EMBER
text: Nope. Nope. Nope! NOPE! NOPE! NOPE!! NOPE!!

speaker: SPARKLER CUSTOMER
text: Just give me one for free!!

speaker: TEENAGE EMBER
text: THAT’S NOT HOW THIS WORKS!! Suddenly, with a handful of sparklers, Ember’s flame turns purple and she EXPLODES. A HUGE KABOOM!

speaker: BERNIE
text: Oh! He grabs a few of the unlit sparklers, sticks them in the basket blob, blows on the them to LIGHT THEM, and hands it to the customer.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Happy birthday! Bernie quickly walks Ember out from behind the counter. Ember is shocked and upset.

speaker: BERNIE
text: What just happened? Why you lose temper?

speaker: TEENAGE EMBER
text: I, I don’t know. He was pushing and pushing and it just--!!

speaker: BERNIE
text: Calm, calm. Sometimes customer can be tough. Just take breath and make connection -- Ember nods, taking this in. Bernie sweetly puts her apron back on.

speaker: BERNIE
text: When you can do that, and not lose temper, then you will be ready to take over shop.

speaker: CUSTOMER
text: ...This is too expensive and that is not made in Fire Land...

speaker: EMBER
text: Take breath, make connection.

speaker: CUSTOMER
text: And they weren’t crunchy and there wasn’t enough sauce. I think this is rotten and you touch everything...

speaker: EMBER
text: Take breath, make connection. Close on Ember’s face, bright red with licks of purple.

speaker: CUSTOMER
text: Do you even have a license for this? Why are you breathing like that?

speaker: EMBER
text: Take breath, make connection. Take breath--

speaker: EMBER
text: --MAKE CONNECTION!!!! KA-BLAM. She EXPLODES. The large glass in the front counter SMASHES to pieces.

speaker: CUSTOMER
text: Ah! EMBER deflates, frustrated, and gives a GENUINE sorry look to shell-shocked customer:

speaker: EMBER
text: Sorry. Sorry about that. Sorry, sorry. The shop regulars, FLARRY and FLARRIETTA, sit at a table with a FIRE DANGER sign. Flarry moves the arrow to the edge of purple.

speaker: FLARRIETTA
text: She almost went full purple! I’ve never seen anyone go full purple!

speaker: EMBER
text: Sorry everyone. Bernie hurries over to the customer.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Oh, please forgive my daughter. She burn bright, but sometimes TOO bright, eh? He blows out a burning flower on the customer’s hat.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Nice hat by the way.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Let me make you a new batch! On the house! The still stunned customer nods and walks away.

speaker: CUSTOMER
text: Okay. Ember jumps over the counter to scoop up all the glass pieces... while she cleans:

speaker: EMBER
text: Sorry Àshfá. I don’t know why that one got away from me. She puts the glass in her mouth and starts chewing. Bernie moves into a station next to the counter that’s filled with coals, he starts making the kol-nuts.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Oh you are tense because of big Red Dot Sale tomorrow. It has us all at a broil. Ember spits out the glass in a long glowing tube that she blows and molds into a pane.

speaker: EMBER
text: I guess. It’s just, some of these customers, they get me all, grrr. She accidentally smooshes the glass in her hands. Oops.

speaker: BERNIE
text: I know, I know. Just do what we practice. You are SO good at everything else.

speaker: EMBER
text: You’re right. I’ll get it. I just want you to rest. Ember blows on the glass, smoothing it into shape. This is a natural talent of her’s and she’s proud of it.

speaker: EMBER
text: Mmhmm. She sets the glass into place.

speaker: EMBER
text: Done. Bernie goes back to making kol-nuts but bursts into a COUGHING fit.

speaker: EMBER
text: You okay?

speaker: BERNIE
text: Just tired.

speaker: EMBER
text: Let me help. She helps him off the coals and climbs in the station to take over.

speaker: FLARRY
text: Bernie, that cough is terrible.

speaker: FLARRIETTA
text: Almost as terrible as your cooking.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ê...shútsh...

speaker: FLARRIETTA
text: When you gonna put Ember out of her misery and retire, huh? Finally put her name on the sign out there?

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ah, she take over when she’s ready. Ember’s face briefly sinks. Not the answer she wanted. She hides the disappointment, puts her shields up and covers:

speaker: EMBER
text: And speaking of “ready,” we are MORE than ready for you to actually BUY something, if you’d ever get up off your lazy ash. The shop reacts with laughter and an “Oh! Burn!”

speaker: FLARRIETTA
text: Burn!

speaker: BERNIE
text: But she is SO close.

speaker: BERNIE
text: I mean, she’ll probably never do deliveries as quick as me... Ember puts the last kol-nut into the basket. She picks up a counter timer and cranks it.

speaker: EMBER
text: You don’t think I can beat your record? Because I’ve been taking it easy on you so I don’t hurt your feelings, Mr. Smokestack.

speaker: EMBER
text: But game on! Bernie watches with a smile as Ember hurries to pack up deliveries.

speaker: CINDER
text: Before I see...

speaker: CINDER
text: ...if you are a match, I splash this on your heart to bring love to the surface. Cinder splashes them with oil.

speaker: CINDER
text: And I will read the smoke. They each light a stick and Cinder studies the smoke. Cinder leans in, excited. Just then, Ember throws open the curtains with a WHOOSH. The smoke dissipates.

speaker: CINDER
text: Ember! I’m doing a reading!

speaker: EMBER
text: Sorry, gotta grab some stuff. Ember starts collecting items.

speaker: EMBER
text: Going for Dad’s record.

speaker: FIRE GUY
text: So... are we a match?

speaker: CINDER
text: It's true love! She turns toward Ember.

speaker: CINDER
text: Which is more than I ever smelled on this one.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh goodie. This ol’ chestnut. As Ember moves toward the exit, Cinder grabs her arm and sniffs it.

speaker: CINDER
text: Yup, nothing. Just a loveless sad future of sadness. Ember turns to leave but her mom stops her again.

speaker: CINDER
text: Ember! Work with me! Ember rolls her eyes.

speaker: CINDER
text: You finding match was my mother's dying wish!

speaker: EMBER
text: Nice try, Mom. Gotta go! Ember exits and Cinder turns back to the couple who are now full-on making out. Cinder picks up a spray bottle and squirts.

speaker: CINDER
text: Save it for the wedding!

speaker: FIRE GUY
text: Hey!

speaker: CLOD
text: Yo yo yo Ember!

speaker: EMBER
text: Yo Clod. Can’t talk, in a hurry. And don’t let my dad catch you out here again. Clod combs his hair with a gardening fork.

speaker: CLOD
text: What? C’mon. He doesn’t like my “laaandscaping?” Ember gives a half-smirk but keeps loading.

speaker: EMBER
text: Uff.

speaker: CLOD
text: Anyway, June Bloom is coming and you just got to be my date. Cause check it out! I’m all grown up! He lifts up his arm revealing a small flower.

speaker: CLOD
text: And I smell goooood. He plucks the armpit flower and holds it out to her, kneeling.

speaker: CLOD
text: Ow! My queen. Ember takes the flower. POOF it burns.

speaker: EMBER
text: Sorry buddy. Elements don’t mix. Ember LOOKS through the front window of the shop at the TIMER on the counter.

speaker: EMBER
text: Flame! Gotta go! She gets back to business. But Clod is persistent.

speaker: CLOD
text: Come on! Go to the festival with me! You never leave this part of town.

speaker: EMBER
text: That’s because everything I need is right here. Just then a WATER TRAIN passes by on an elevated track. Water splashes and Ember swiftly pops open her umbrella. She looks at the train crossing the bridge, annoyed. By contrast, Clod happily soaks up the shower.

speaker: EMBER
text: Plus the city isn’t made with Fire people in mind. Irritated, she closes her umbrella and hops on her scooter.

speaker: EMBER
text: Sorry, but it’d take an act of God to get me to cross that bridge.

speaker: CLOD
text: An act of God or an act of... Clod? Ember starts her scooter and pulls away.

speaker: EMBER
text: Gotta run! She drives off.

speaker: EMBER
text: As ordered. They lift the lid off the grill, revealing a tiny FIRE BABY keeping warm over the charcoals. One of the parents feeds the baby the bottle. The baby sucks on it and burbs flames.

speaker: EMBER
text: Gotta run! Going for dad’s record. -- Ember is behind a SLOW old truck carrying wood. She loses her patience.

speaker: EMBER
text: Move it! She manuevers her scooter around the truck. She turns back, shakes her fist and calls back.

speaker: EMBER
text: Sháshà r íshà! -- SHOTS CUTTING FASTER -- Ember delivers sandbags to a SMOKE CLEANERS experiencing a water leak. -- She hands off a parcel outside a WOODSHOP. -- She races by a FIREWORKS COMPANY dropping off a box of FIREWORKS. Kids swarm around the box, the contents explode and they cheer. Ember drives off with the fireworks going off behind her. The sequence ends with Ember racing home, happy, free.

speaker: EMBER
text: Ha ha! Winner winner charcoal dinner-- She sees something and quickly catches the door so it won’t slam. We reveal Bernie asleep at the counter, pen in hand and surrounded by sheets of red dot stickers. Ember softly shuts the door and tip toes in. She smiles lovingly, then cleans up the papers and drapes a chainmail shawl over his shoulders. He wakes with a SMOKE COUGH. Ember gently pulls a red dot sticker off his cheek.

speaker: EMBER
text: Head to bed. I’ll close things up. Bernie gets up and Ember follows.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Still have much to prepare for Red Dot Sale.

speaker: EMBER
text: Dad, I'll take care of it. You need to rest. Just then the TIMER rings. Bernie looks at the timer, then to Ember with excitement.

speaker: BERNIE
text: How?

speaker: EMBER
text: I learned from the best. They share a warm laugh. Ember puts a hand on Bernie’s back and leads him across the shop. As they approach the Blue Flame, Bernie starts coughing again.

speaker: BERNIE
text: I am old. I can’t do this forever. He picks up a stick from the base of the Blue Flame’s cauldron.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Now that you have beaten my time there is only one thing you haven’t done... tomorrow I sleep in. And I want YOU to run shop for Red Dot sale.

speaker: EMBER
text: Seriously?? By myself??

speaker: BERNIE
text: If you can do that without losing your temper, it will show me you are able to take over. He breaks the twig in two and hands a piece to Ember.

speaker: EMBER
text: You got it Àshfá. Bernie holds the twig with both hands and closes his eyes. Then he tosses it into the Blue Flame. Ember beams.

speaker: EMBER
text: I won’t let you down. I swear. You’ll see. They share a warm hug.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Hmm. Good daughter. Bernie slowly climbs the stairs. Ember watches him go, then flares up, excited.

speaker: EMBER
text: Yes! Ember turns to the Blue Flame. Just like Bernie, she holds her stick and closes her eyes before tossing it into the cauldron.

speaker: EMBER
text: Blue Flame, please let this go my way.

speaker: EMBER
text: Take a breath. Calm as a candle. She rolls up the front window shade and sees a wall of customers waiting to come in. Her eyes go wide for a moment but then she puts on a smile and opens the door.

speaker: EMBER
text: Morning. Welcome to the Fireplace-- Customers RUSH in and she’s practically trampled. We go to a series of POPS of the sale. It’s chaotic, like Black Friday. Ember fights to keep her cool. -- A CUSTOMER yanks on a can at the bottom of a pyramid display, shaking the whole stack. Ember rushes up.

speaker: EMBER
text: Whoa whoa, they’re all the same. Just take one from the top. Thanks for shopping. -- A CUSTOMER heaves an armload of red stickers onto the counter

speaker: STICKER CUSTOMER
text: So many stickers for sale! -- A customer sweeps a whole shelf of Blue Flame trinkets into a basket.

speaker: TRINKET CUSTOMER
text: Are these fragile?

speaker: EMBER
text: No wait! SMASH. Several trinkets fall and break. -- Ember stops a CUSTOMER from putting a log in their mouth:

speaker: EMBER
text: You have to pay before you eat. -- A crescendo of SUPER QUICK SHOTS of Ember at the register. All she can see is faces and questions and items being shoved toward her.

speaker: CUSTOMERS
text: Does this come in a large? / What’s your return policy? / These glasses are backward. / I want a refund. / I’m next!! / Has anyone seen my husband? INTERCUT TO CLOSE UPS OF EMBER TRYING TO BREATH THROUGH

speaker: EMBER
text: Take breath. A kid holds up a broken toy.

speaker: KID CUSTOMER
text: My dad broke this.

speaker: EMBER
text: Make connection... A high-pitched kettle starts to whistle.

speaker: KETTLE CUSTOMER
text: Mind if I test this kettle? It’s too much. Ember is on the verge of EXPLODING. Smoke building and fire roiling. With gritted teeth and a SUPER STRAINED voice, her color starts turning more purple...

speaker: EMBER
text: Be back in 5 minutes! She rushes awkwardly out, sparks and flame blasting, hands covering her mouth, barely contained.

speaker: EMBER
text: AHHHHHH! Her fire BLASTS. As the smoke clears, there’s a LOUD VIBRATION from a pipe. CUT TO upstairs, the pipes in the shop are vibrating too. The customers look around, concerned. Back in the basement, the pipe cracks and SHOOTS a fire-hoselike stream of water right at Ember! It douses part of her flame and she ducks away. More streams burst from the pipe, spraying all over the basement. Ember panics. Water is SPRAYING EVERYWHERE, filling the basement and HURTING HER. She has to dodge the spray. Thinking quick, she picks up an umbrella and, getting STUNG by water, jumps toward the pipe. She uses her hands to heat the pipe and weld the crack, stopping the leak. Then, she surveys the damage. The basement is completely flooded.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh no no no no no. Stupid temper! Not today! Ember’s flame is damaged. She quickly grabs sticks from a bucket and eats them, growing her flame.

speaker: EMBER
text: What is wrong with me? Just then, A PICTURE FRAME floats to the surface of the water and starts to move. Then two streams of water fountain up out of the pool and two hands holding the frame appear... WADE sits up out of the water, bawling his eyes out.

speaker: EMBER
text: What the--? He looks at the picture, moved, crying.

speaker: WADE
text: What a happy family. Is that you and your DAD?? The frame holds a photo of young Ember and her parents. She is held up by Bernie, blowing fire on a birthday cake, the candles sparking.

speaker: WADE
text: I love dads. And it’s your birthday! His tears splash Ember.

speaker: EMBER
text: Who are you? What are you doing here?

speaker: WADE
text: I don’t know! I was searching for a leak on the other side of the river and got sucked in. This is bad! I can’t lose another job! I just can’t seem to find my flow. He stands, revealing himself for the first time. And he’s HUNKY, CUT. Ember can’t help but notice.

speaker: EMBER
text: Dang. Then Wade looks down at himself.

speaker: WADE
text: Ugh, that pipe squished me all out of shape. He shakes and PLOPS back into his regular bouncy Wade shape.

speaker: WADE
text: That’s better.

speaker: EMBER
text: Dude, just get out of here. I gotta clean this mess before my dad sees what I did.

speaker: WADE
text: Ooo, actually... Wade sloshes his way over to a junction of several pipes. Frowning, her pulls a notepad and pen out from under his shirt.

speaker: WADE
text: I’m afraid I’m going to have to write you a ticket. He starts scribbling on the pad.

speaker: EMBER
text: A ticket??

speaker: WADE
text: Yeah. I’m a city inspector. And this pipe is definitely not up to code.

speaker: EMBER
text: I sucked a city inspector into our pipes??

speaker: WADE
text: I know, ironic, right? Wade taps on the pipe.

speaker: EMBER
text: Stop messing with that!

speaker: WADE
text: I need to make sure it’s solid.

speaker: EMBER
text: Everything’s solid. I should know. My dad re-built this place himself.

speaker: WADE
text: Wait, your dad did?

speaker: EMBER
text: Yes! With his bare hands. Every brick and board. It was a ruin when he found it.

speaker: WADE
text: Wow. He did all of this himself? Without permits?

speaker: EMBER
text: Uh... Wade’s tears start to flow again.

speaker: WADE
text: I’m gonna have to write that up too. First I’m sucked into a pipe and now I have to write citations that could get this place shut down. Oh gosh, it’s just too much! Ember instantly FLAMES UP.

speaker: EMBER
text: Shut us DOWN??

speaker: WADE
text: I know! It’s awful!

speaker: EMBER
text: No! You can’t shut us down. Please! This is a big day for me. It’s our Red Dot Sale! Ember flares her arms out trying to grab the ticket book.

speaker: WADE
text: Hey, take it easy. This is as hard on me on as it is on you.

speaker: EMBER
text: Get back here!! Wade runs for the other side of the room.

speaker: WADE
text: Sorry! I gotta get these to city hall before the end of my shift. He slips out a basement window. Ember lunges after him. She looks at her Red Dot Sale button then rips off her apron, tosses it on the ground.

speaker: EMBER
text: Flame!

speaker: EMBER
text: Get back here! REVEAL Bernie looking out the window. He sees Ember chase Wade down the street.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Hmm?

speaker: TRAIN ANNOUNCER
text: Next stop, Element City. Down the platform, Wade, not seeing Ember, steps into a waiting train. She has to follow. Crap. She looks back at Firetown. As the doors start to close, Ember throws on her hood and SLIPS into the last car.

speaker: EMBER
text: Whoa! She slams into the grass man. POOF his grass burns up leaving a very skinny guy.

speaker: EARTH ELEMENT
text: Hay!

speaker: EMBER
text: Sorry! She looks ahead, there’s Wade. But it’s too crowded for her to squeeze through. There has to be another way. Thinking fast, she climbs out a window.

speaker: EMBER
text: Huh? She moves closer... could it be her own reflection?

speaker: EMBER
text: What the? Just then the train exits the tunnel and the car is bright again and she sees that what she was looking at was her reflection was on the back of Wade’s head! But she’s got the tickets in sight. They’re in his hand. She’s so close. She slowly reaches toward them, but her heat causes Wade’s hand to quietly BOIL...

speaker: WADE
text: Ah ah ah! Hands off! He quickly whips the tickets away from her and hurries out the open doors.

speaker: EMBER
text: Gah! She follows but has to dodge passengers loading onto the train.

speaker: EMBER
text: Ooh, sorry.

speaker: EMBER
text: Ugh. Then she spots Wade at the bottom of the stairs, headed toward a busy street. He weaves easily through the crowds.

speaker: EMBER
text: Stop!! The chase is on. Ember rushes down a busy staircase... she flails, collides with other people and tumbles down the steps into a lamp post. But she’s not giving up. She leaps to her feet.

speaker: EMBER
text: Stop!! She gets to the busy street and stops for traffic just as an AIR ELEMENT steps in front of a car. POOF! He’s hit, then reforms, his jacket laying in the street.

speaker: AIR ELEMENT
text: Aw! My new jacket. Ember bolts across the street.

speaker: EARTH KIDS.
text: Ember follows, but the kids are blocking her path. Thinking fast, she pops open her umbrella and flares up. Like a hot air balloon, she lifts up and over the kids. They’re amazed. She keeps soaring until she passes over the EARTH KIDS. Suddenly, her umbrella melts and gives out. She falls to the ground landing hard, then springs to her feet and keeps chasing Wade who now realizes he’s being chased. Yikes! He takes a sharp left, appearing to splash into the side of a building. Ember skids to a stop and sees he’s slipped into a very narrow space between two buildings. She squeezes her fire and follows him. Crammed, they both shimmy through the extremely tight passageway. Wade makes it to the other side but his ticket book gets stuck in the crack. He desperately pulls and gets it free JUST as Ember bursts from the crack and swipes for the ticket book. But Wade gets away and darts through traffic toward City Hall as Ember follows.

speaker: DIRT BURGER VENDOR
text: My chili oil! Ember makes it to City Hall first and jumps in front of Wade’s path, exhausted. She sprays the oil in a line in front of her then stomps on it creating a HUGE WALL OF FIRE.

speaker: EMBER
text: Come on, guy. You can’t get through this. So it is time to hand ‘em over.

speaker: WADE
text: Oh boy. I’m sorry. This is going to be really disappointing for you-- Wade steps onto a drain and PLOP disappears, then pops up through another grate on the other side of Ember right next to the building. He hurries inside.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh no no no no no no... But he rushes into a revolving door.

speaker: EMBER
text: No no no no no no!

speaker: WADE
text: Sorry! And slips into the building. Ember’s flame shrinks in defeat.

speaker: EMBER
text: Please! No! You don’t understand. Ember slumps on the steps, her fire dims and reveals an inner prismatic light. ON THE OTHER SIDE OF THE DOORS, Wade puts the tickets in a canister. But just as he’s putting the canister into a vacuum

speaker: WADE
text: Whoa. He turns to see Ember outside, desperate, pleading to the closed door, more to herself, her light private, prismatic, vulnerable.

speaker: EMBER
text: The shop is my dad’s dream, if I’m the reason it gets shut down it will kill him. INSIDE, Wade’s face looks pained, empathetic.

speaker: WADE
text: Aw.

speaker: EMBER
text: He will never trust me to take over. Wade comes out of the building.

speaker: WADE
text: Why didn’t you say that before? Ember INSTANTLY ZIPS her fire up, shields on.

speaker: EMBER
text: Wait, does that mean you’ll tear up the tickets?

speaker: WADE
text: I mean, I would. But I just sent them over to the processing department. Wade gestures to the root vacuum system inside the doors. Ember grips her head in frustration.

speaker: WADE
text: But, I can take you there so you can plead your case. Ember looks back at him, hopeful again.

speaker: EMBER
text: Whoa! They break through to find an overgrown Earth Element, FERN, a low-key humorless bureaucrat with PILES of paper in his inbox. Behind his desk are a series of vacuum tubes made from VINES.

speaker: WADE
text: Hey, Fern! How you doing?

speaker: FERN
text: Living the dream. Fern inserts a roll of papers into a canister.

speaker: WADE
text: You know those citations I just gave you from Firetown?

speaker: FERN
text: I was about to send them to Mrs. Cumulus then get sprayed for fungus rot. Fern moves to put the canister into the tube.

speaker: EMBER
text: Wait!

speaker: WADE
text: Wait!

speaker: WADE
text: Tell him what you told me. About your dad and letting him down.

speaker: EMBER
text: No. That’s personal.

speaker: WADE
text: It really got to me. He might feel it too... Wade keepings talking as Ember tries to cover his mouth.

speaker: WADE
text: Her dad will be sup--

speaker: EMBER
text: Nope!

speaker: WADE
text: Sup--

speaker: EMBER
text: Nope!

speaker: WADE
text: Super disa-- She then grabs Fern’s nameplate and, SPLTT!, shoves it into Wade’s mouth.

speaker: WADE
text: --ppointed in her. Ember is triggered by Wade exposing her.

speaker: EMBER
text: Stop it! Wade keeps talking with the nameplate in his head.

speaker: WADE
text: He might even be... Ember shoves a globe into his face, knocking out the nameplate...

speaker: WADE
text: ...ASHAMED.

speaker: EMBER
text: What are you doing? Ember’s flame starts to show some PURPLE. She starts stress breathing.

speaker: WADE
text: But the main thing is if her father can’t retire... Fern moves the canister closer to the tube.

speaker: WADE
text: ...It will be ALL EMBER’S.. FFFFEMBER CUTS HIM OFF!

speaker: EMBER
text: STOP TALKING! Ember EXPLODES in a ball of fire. When the smoke clears we see ALL of the stacks of citations have been fried. Except Ember’s that are in the canister. Wade douses a still flaming bobble head. Fern’s charred desk collapses.

speaker: FERN
text: Looks like I’m going home early today. He puts the canister into the tube.

speaker: EMBER
text: No, don’t-- The tickets shoot out of the office. Failure.

speaker: FERN
text: Expect to get shut down within a week. Have a good one. Fern hands Ember a brochure. She takes it and turns away, dejected. Wade watches, concerned.

speaker: WADE
text: Sorry.

speaker: EMBER
text: What?!

speaker: EMBER
text: Already?? She looks around the empty shop and tucks the brochure into her pocket.

speaker: EMBER
text: Hello? She hears Bernie coughing in the basement.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh no. Dad! What happened??

speaker: BERNIE
text: We lucky nobody hurt. It RUIN Red Dot Sale! Did he do this?

speaker: EMBER
text: Who?

speaker: BERNIE
text: The Water guy I see you chase. Water drips onto Bernie's face.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ah!

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh, uh, um... Ember weighs this for a moment, as she hops off the stairs and begins repairing a pipe. She could come clean. But instead...

speaker: EMBER
text: Yeah. He did. Uh, he just broke through a pipe. I don’t know why. Luckily I was able close it off. I, uh, couldn’t catch him though. Bernie flares up.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Water. Always trying to water us down!

speaker: EMBER
text: He was a Water PERSON, dad, not just water.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Same thing. And why is water in pipes? City shut down years ago. There should be NO WATER! Bernie’s fury causes a coughing fit. He falls over backward.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ah!

speaker: EMBER
text: Dad!!! Ember catches him just before he hits the water.

speaker: CINDER
text: Bernie! Bernie coughs. Cinder leads him to the stairs.

speaker: CINDER
text: We will get through this. Just like before.

speaker: EMBER
text: Before? Bernie sits, Cinder looks to Ember.

speaker: CINDER
text: There is a reason we left Fire Land. Cinder holds Bernie’s arm, she wants to talk about it. Bernie looks down. Cinder smiles at a memory. She’s almost talking MORE to Bernie, reassuring him they did

speaker: CINDER
text: Oh, Ember. We loved it there so much. Most everyone had a Blue Flame, and it connected us all together...

speaker: CINDER
text: ...to our traditions, our family. The camera passes ground-floor windows and we get FLEETING GLIMPSES of FAMILY FLAMES, until we stop at a window of a FAMILY SHOP that has a Blue Flame burning in a cauldron inside. A YOUNG BERNIE stands on a ladder, putting up a sign. He looks to a VERY PREGNANT Cinder who smiles, so proud.

speaker: CINDER
text: It was hard living, but your father began to build a life for us. We put everything into it. But then a great storm came. There’s a PULSE of wind and Bernie and Cinder’s flames flicker ominously. Worried, they look up to the sky and see a large dark storm above the rooftops. And then it hits them --

speaker: CINDER
text: All was lost for us. LATER that night, Cinder sleeps on the floor, but Bernie is awake. He looks to his lantern with the Blue Flame he saved and prays to it then looks to pregnant Cinder.

speaker: CINDER
text: Your father understood... We HAD to leave... everything. Our home.

speaker: CINDER
text: It was the only way to create a better life! As they board, Bernie looks back at his parents. Stoic, they watch him leave. The boat pulls away from the shore and Bernie and Cinder watch their homeland recede in the distance.

speaker: CINDER
text: It was the last time your father ever saw his family.

speaker: CINDER
text: That is why we came here. To build all this. Ember is floored. She looks at the brochure from Fern, then tucks it away and walks over to Bernie with resolve:

speaker: EMBER
text: Áshfá... nothing will happen to this shop or the flame again. I promise. Ember takes the mop from her father. He smiles and places his hand on her cheek.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Mm. Good daughter.

speaker: WADE
text: Ah! Fire. Fire! Ah! Fire! He pats out his bag, then starts to stomp on the fire. Suddenly EMBER stands up, emerging from under a chainmail blanket... the fire was her.

speaker: EMBER
text: Hey HEY!

speaker: WADE
text: Oh. Sorry! You’re so hot!

speaker: EMBER
text: Excuse me?

speaker: WADE
text: No! I mean like you’re smokin’! No, I didn’t mean it like tha--

speaker: EMBER
text: Are you done yet? Wade is dripping.

speaker: WADE
text: Yes please.

speaker: EMBER
text: I’m waiting to talk to your boss. So make like a stream and... flow somewhere else. Ember settles back down on the ground.

speaker: WADE
text: Actually, Gale won’t be in today. She’s a HUGE airball fan and the Windbreakers are finally in the playoffs! Toot Toot! Ember is unaffected by Wade’s enthusiasm. She flares up in frustration.

speaker: EMBER
text: UGH!

speaker: WADE
text: Okay... Well, I just came by because I left my passes for the game here last night. Wade starts to walk away but Ember perks up.

speaker: EMBER
text: Passes? Like, plural? He turns back. Then it hits him, is she serious?

speaker: EMBER
text: Where is she?

speaker: WADE
text: Up there, in that skybox. Wade points to one of the sky boxes where GALE, Wade’s supervisor, sits. She’s a storm cloud, complete with lightning, and is completely engrossed in the game.

speaker: GALE
text: Come on!

speaker: EMBER
text: Okay.

speaker: EMBER
text: Time to cancel some tickets. They walk towards Gale’s box, passing a drink vendor.

speaker: TOOT TOOT JUICE VENDOR
text: Toot toot juice! Getcha toot toot juice! Toot juice! Getcha toot toot juice! They get to the row behind Gale’s box. Everyone sitting there is Water.

speaker: WADE
text: You’ll be great! This way. Wade walks down the row. Everyone seems to know him. Ember

speaker: EMBER
text: Excuse me. Sorry. Pardon. Oh sorry. Fire girl coming through.

speaker: WADE
text: Jimmy, what’s up? Wendy! How good is it to be here?

speaker: GALE
text: Break some wind!!

speaker: WADE
text: Hi Gale. How you doing? Gale glances back at her.

speaker: GALE
text: Look at the score. What do you think?? She turns back to the game with a rumble.

speaker: GALE
text: Blow the BALL not the GAME!

speaker: EMBER
text: Yeah, so uh, Gale. My name is Ember Lumen. My family runs a Fire shop... Wade wrote us a bunch of tickets yesterday and-- BUZZER! Game going south. Gale gets stormier, her winds blow in Ember and Wade’s faces.

speaker: GALE
text: What kind of call was that??!! Lumen? Yeah, Fire shop with thirty citations...

speaker: EMBER
text: Thirty?? Wade shrugs apologetically. Ember turns back to Gale.

speaker: EMBER
text: Anyway, friend, I was hoping we could work something out-- BUZZER again! Gale erupts.

speaker: GALE
text: Come on Ref! ARE YOUR EYES IN THE BACK OF YOUR HEAD?? The AIR REF’S eyes literally pop out the back of his head as he GLARES at Gale.

speaker: WADE
text: Oh no! But not reading the mood, Ember tries again.

speaker: EMBER
text: Yeah bummer, aww yeah. Okay, so the 30 citations-- Gale whips around to face Ember, annoyed.

speaker: GALE
text: Do you mind? There’s a GAME going on. As she turns back toward the game,

speaker: GALE
text: Fireball. This gets to Ember. Her flame starts to spark and roil.

speaker: EMBER
text: Fireball? She steps in front of Gale’s box, between her and the game.

speaker: EMBER
text: Actually, I do mind. This is my LIFE we’re talking about. Not just some game. Gale THUNDERS huge with LIGHTNING.

speaker: GALE
text: “Some game?” This is the PLAYOFFS. So forgive me if I don’t want to hear a sob story about the problems of some little shop. Ember’s flames get bigger too. We see licks of PURPLE.

speaker: EMBER
text: Well that “little shop” matters WAY more than a bunch of over-paid cloud puffs blowing some ball around. Ember and Gale are nose to nose.

speaker: GALE
text: I dare you. Say “cloud puffs” one more time.

speaker: EMBER
text: Cloud. Puffs. Ember blows away Gale’s nose. Gale furious, growls back. Ember’s fire ROARS. They angrily stare each other down as tension quickly builds. The crowd boos.

speaker: WADE
text: Oh no! Gale’s eyes dart to the game. Huh?? Ember looks to the game.

speaker: WADE
text: Lutz! LUTZ, one of the players, tries to block the play but fails. The home team is scored on again. As the crowd boos, Lutz’s spirit sinks.

speaker: WADE
text: Lutz man, he’s been in such a funk ‘cause his mom has been sick. Lutz has the ball stolen from him. The crowd keeps booing.

speaker: WADE
text: That is so not cool. He’s doing his best. Wade suddenly stands and shouts.

speaker: WADE
text: We love you Lutz! Ember ducks and looks away from him.

speaker: WADE
text: LUUUUTZ! Wade starts gesturing to the fans around him to join the chant.

speaker: WADE
text: We love you Lutz! We love you Lutz! C’mon! We love you Lutz! Everybody!

speaker: CROWD
text: We love you Lutz! We love you Lutz! We love you Lutz! We love you Lutz!

speaker: WADE
text: We love you Lutz! We love you Lutz! We love you Lutz! We love you Lutz! Lutz looks over at their section, clearly touched. The whole stadium picks up the cheer. Then Wade starts an ACTUAL WAVE of Water People that goes all the way around the stadium.

speaker: WADE
text: Whooooooaaaaa...

speaker: CROWD
text: Whooooooaaaaa... Ember opens her umbrella just as the wave SPLASHES OVER them. ON THE COURT, Lutz is energized. He blasts up to the top of the stadium and SLAMS the ball into the net for a score. The crowd goes wild. Ember even gets caught up in the moment. It’s a stadium ELECTRIFIED!

speaker: EMBER
text: Ah, yes! Yes!

speaker: WADE
text: Woo! Way to go Lutz! Lutz holds his arms up - success!

speaker: WADE
text: Yeah Lutz! That was amazing Lutz! Wade high-fives everyone around them.

speaker: WADE
text: Uh! Yeah! Yeah! Yes! Ember looks at Wade, amazed at what he just did.

speaker: WADE
text: Woo hoo! Wade tries to high five her, she instinctively leans away.

speaker: WADE
text: Oh. He high fives himself.

speaker: GALE
text: Wohoo! What a comeback! REVEAL Wade covered head to toe in Windbreakers merch, including two foam hands, a puffy wig and cloud-shaped glasses.

speaker: WADE
text: Check out who found the gift shop! Woo! Ember does a double-take at Wade with a smile... this guy is ridiculous, then to Gale:

speaker: EMBER
text: I gotta admit that WAS pretty cool.

speaker: WADE
text: Uh! Yeah!

speaker: GALE
text: You can see why I can get all churned up. But as a “cloud puff” who used to come here with her dad, these wins mean a little bit more.

speaker: EMBER
text: And as a “Fireball” who’s supposed to take over her dad’s shop... ...I sure don’t want to let him down... and I could use a win too. Ember’s letting her guard down a little.

speaker: EMBER
text: Now I just gotta stop water from coming in--

speaker: GALE
text: WATER? In FIRETOWN?

speaker: EMBER
text: Yeah?

speaker: GALE
text: Water was shut off to there YEARS ago. Forget the tickets, I’m gonna have to TAKE APART YOUR DAD’S SHOP to figure out what’s going on!!

speaker: EMBER
text: YOU CAN’T! My dad put his WHOLE LIFE into that place!

speaker: GALE
text: ARGG. I bet this is connected to that fluffin’ leak.

speaker: WADE
text: Yeah, we’ve been trying to track down a leak in the city. It’s why I was in the canal and--WAIT!! I know where I got sucked into Ember’s shop! Ember and I could track the water from her shop to find the SOURCE OF THE LEAK!

speaker: GALE
text: Keep talking.

speaker: WADE
text: I could call in a city crew to fix whatever we find.

speaker: EMBER
text: Yes! And there’d be no need to touch my dad’s shop! Gale thinks, then smiles at their hopeful faces.

speaker: GALE
text: You’re lucky you’re a cute couple.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh, we’re not a--

speaker: GALE
text: You got until FRIDAY. If you can find the leak and get a crew to fix it by then those tickets are forgiven. If not? YOUR DAD’S SHOP gets shut down. Ember feels the weight of this. Gale flies up into the flowing air crowd, hooting, cheering:

speaker: GALE
text: WOOHOO! BREAK WIND! BREAK WIND!

speaker: EMBER
text: Thank you! Ember exhales... she got a reprieve. She looks to Wade.

speaker: EMBER
text: Please take all that off.

speaker: WADE
text: But I got you a hat! Wade puts the hat on Ember’s head. We go wide to see a huge plume of fire and smoke explode above her. Back on them, that hat is gone and Wade’s outfit is singed.

speaker: WADE
text: Okay.

speaker: EMBER
text: Just keep outta sight, okay? It'd be a whole thing. They hear Bernie from inside the shop, he’s livid.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Now water upstairs?? It’s in the walls. Ember and Wade peer through a window. They watch as Bernie rips away a piece of wall revealing a leaking pipe.

speaker: BERNIE
text: I don’t understand! I fix one pipe and another one leaks! Bernie pushes the Blue Flame away from the leak.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ah, water! Bernie throws open the window. Coughing and letting smoke out. Ember and Wade plaster themselves to the wall. Bernie leaves the window.

speaker: EMBER
text: How could it be worse?

speaker: WADE
text: Now that water’s back, the pressure is forcing it up to ALL your pipes.

speaker: EMBER
text: We gotta find the source! They crouch down and peer into the basement.

speaker: EMBER
text: How did you even end up here? We follow a pipe going from the shop to the culvert and into the floor of the canal.

speaker: WADE
text: Well, I was in the canals checking the doors for leaks...

speaker: WADE
text: When I found some water that shouldn’t have been there... He dips his finger in the puddle and tastes the water...

speaker: WADE
text: ...rusty, with a hint of-- motor oil? SUDDENLY there is a RUSH of water. Wade is SWEPT OFF HIS FEET and SUCKED into a drain.

speaker: WADE
text: There was this WHOOSH of water.

speaker: WADE
text: AHHHH!

speaker: WADE
text: And I got sucked into a filtering system... Wade is knocked around a filtering system.

speaker: WADE
text: Help! THEN BAM! He’s JAMMED into a pipe that’s clogged with DEBRIS.

speaker: WADE
text: But then I heard this explosion... SUDDENLY there’s the VIBRATION that shook the pipe when Ember exploded.

speaker: EMBER
text: AHHHHHH! Wade’s eyes grow in terror as he ALSO vibrates. The debris breaks up and FOOMP he’s sucked in.

speaker: WADE
text: AHHHH! On Wade’s face as he bursts out of a pipe. Behind him we see, through the distortion of the water, a blurry Ember...

speaker: WADE
text: That’s how I ended up at your place.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh flame, my temper caused this. Ember looks down the seemingly endless canals.

speaker: EMBER
text: So we’re searching for water “somewhere” in a canal?? Those canals go EVERYWHERE.

speaker: WADE
text: It’s why tracking down that leak has been so dang hard.

speaker: EMBER
text: The roof.

speaker: EMBER
text: You might want to step back. She climbs into the cap and flares up super bright and hot. The tarp inflates like a hot air balloon. Wade is astonished.

speaker: WADE
text: Holy dew drop!

speaker: EMBER
text: Shh. Get in. Wade scurries and climbs in as it starts to RISE. He stares at Ember with WONDER. They float above the rooftops of Firetown. It’s gorgeous. Wade admires Ember’s blaze. They’re so close, his arm starts to boil. He takes a step away from her. We see the spot he was sucked in, there’s a puddle.

speaker: WADE
text: Okay that’s where I got sucked in. The camera PANS up the canal, following a trail of puddles.

speaker: WADE
text: More water. Go that way! Ember turns the balloon to follow the puddles. They pass a building... we hear giggles.

speaker: EARTH PRUNER 1
text: Right there. As they pass a darkened window, Ember’s light illuminates two EARTH ELEMENTS picking each other’s fruit. They FREEZE, caught.

speaker: EARTH PRUNER 2
text: Oh!

speaker: EARTH PRUNER 1
text: Nothing weird going on here.

speaker: EARTH PRUNER 2
text: Uh, just a little pruning. Wade and Ember stare as they float away. After a beat they turn to each other and burst out laughing. A moment of connection. Wade looks at Ember’s amazing fire. She catches him, her light refracting in his water.

speaker: WADE
text: So, uh, what do you do at the shop if you don’t mind me asking?

speaker: EMBER
text: My dad’s retiring and I’ll be taking over. Some day, when I’m ready. They float past glittering skyscrapers.

speaker: WADE
text: It must be nice knowing what you’re gonna do. After my dad passed I got all “what’s the point?” Now I just go from one job to the next. Ember gives him a sympathetic look.

speaker: EMBER
text: There’s a word in Firish. Tìshók'. It means embrace the light while it burns, cause it won’t always last forever. Wade processes and tries to repeat it.

speaker: WADE
text: Tee shook...

speaker: EMBER
text: Or something like that. BUT, just then, they round a large building revealing an older section of town. Ember sees a large, ornate but rundown building. Her face drops. Wade see it.

speaker: WADE
text: You okay?

speaker: EMBER
text: Yeah.

speaker: WADE
text: You sure? She decides to open up, and points to the building.

speaker: EMBER
text: It’s just... that building over there? That’s Garden Central Station.

speaker: EMBER
text: When I was a kid, my dad took me there because they had a VIVISTERIA tree. I’d ALWAYS wanted to see one. It’s the only flower that can thrive in ANY environment. Fire included. They get in line with other ELEMENTS, eager for the exhibit.

speaker: EMBER
text: I was so excited. They approach the doors. But before they enter a GUARD stops them.

speaker: EMBER
text: But they said our Fire was too dangerous and they wouldn’t let us in. The guard points to a “NO FIRE ALLOWED” SIGN. Bernie is FURIOUS. He yells and the guard yells back.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Tsh’à ts’ shâ sh pfùkh!

speaker: GUARD
text: Go back to Fire Land! EVERYBODY laughs. Some jeer.

speaker: MUSEUM GOERS
text: Burn somewhere else! / Get outta here!

speaker: EMBER
text: My dad was so angry, and embarrassed.

speaker: EMBER
text: The building flooded a few years later. So I missed my one chance to see a Vivisteria. She turns to Wade. And to her surprise, he’s tearing up.

speaker: WADE
text: You must have been SO scared. This takes Ember back, and momentarily disarms her. Almost as a kid she says:

speaker: EMBER
text: I was. THEN, she shakes it off, almost annoyed and amazed.

speaker: EMBER
text: How do you do that?

speaker: WADE
text: Do what?

speaker: EMBER
text: Draw people in! You got a whole stadium to connect with you. I-- I can’t even connect with ONE customer. My stupid temper always kicks in.

speaker: WADE
text: I guess I just say what I feel. Ember deflates --this does not resonate at all.

speaker: WADE
text: And I don’t think a temper is so bad. Sometimes when I lose MY temper... I think it’s just ME trying to tell me something I’m not ready to hear.

speaker: EMBER
text: That’s ridiculous.

speaker: WADE
text: Maybe... Suddenly Wade sees something! His eyes GROW with excitement. He points!

speaker: WADE
text: Hey, there! Put us down there!

speaker: WADE
text: That’s not right. Wade dips his finger in some standing water and tastes it.

speaker: WADE
text: Motor oil! Yup, this is the source! They walk through the doors to investigate and head toward the main canal.

speaker: EMBER
text: Why’s there no water?

speaker: WADE
text: Because the doors are broken. This is supposed to catch spillover from those main canals and-- Just then a giant cruise-liner rolls by and it’s wake triggers a small tsunami over the canal walls.

speaker: WADE
text: --RUN FOR YOUR LIFE!! Ember and Wade run toward the culvert doors. Ember jumps through and climbs the door to RELATIVE safety, but Wade is caught on the other side, holding desperately to a broken part of the door.

speaker: WADE
text: Ahhhh! Help! Ahhhh!!! Thinking fast, Ember melts a piece of rebar off the door.

speaker: EMBER
text: Uh... She thrusts it toward him.

speaker: EMBER
text: Grab this! With great effort, Ember pulls Wade to safety.

speaker: EMBER
text: Firetown! She spots a pile of SANDBAGS on the raised edge of the canal. Without hesitation she leaps over the water and climbs to the bags, grabs one and calls out to Wade:

speaker: EMBER
text: Catch!! She swings the sandbag and throws it down to him. Wade turns just as the bag arrives BAM, knocking him to the ground. He gets up and, moving against the flow of water, carries the sandbag toward the doors. Ember lifts another. Water is still pouring through the gates. Ember stops to watch Wade. He holds a sand bag under one arm and uses his free arm to press against the deluge. His body undulates and he starts to grow in size as water fills him up! With super strength, he pushes back the flow. Ember watches from above, impressed. Wade heaves the sand bag at the base of the door opening. He’s now ENORMOUS, waterlogged but powerful. He calls up to Ember.

speaker: WADE
text: Ember! Throw me more! They start to pile more sandbags. Ember throwing them down and Wade stacking them up. When the pile is high and the water stopped, they place the last bag together. They finish... Ember, looks at the sandbags, concerned.

speaker: EMBER
text: So, will this hold? Wade pushes against the bags, testing the strength.

speaker: WADE
text: Yup. He turns to Ember. We see he has a clump of sand in his face. She can’t help but look at it as he talks:

speaker: WADE
text: It should for sure. At least long enough for me to get a city crew to fix it before Friday. What?

speaker: EMBER
text: You’ve got a little... sand. Wade touches his face.

speaker: WADE
text: Oh. Here? Here? Ember points and reaches toward him, she almost touches his cheek.

speaker: EMBER
text: It’s.. right there. Ember pulls her hand away.

speaker: EMBER
text: Um... Wade reaches into his face and pulls it out.

speaker: WADE
text: Oh. Thanks. They keep eye contact. It’s almost intimate. Ember breaks away from it.

speaker: EMBER
text: Well... let me know when it’s done I guess.

speaker: WADE
text: I’ll make sure there’s a city crew here by Friday.

speaker: EMBER
text: Okay, see ya. She turns and starts to walk off. Wade blurts out after:

speaker: WADE
text: Wait! Ember turns back.

speaker: WADE
text: Any chance you’re free tomorrow? To hang out with a Water guy?

speaker: EMBER
text: With a Water guy? My dad would boil you alive.

speaker: WADE
text: He doesn’t have to know! We could meet in the city. I promise nothing weird... maybe a little pruning?

speaker: EMBER
text: Sorry. That’s not going to happen. ON Ember as Wade, hopeful, watches her walk out of frame.

speaker: WADE
text: You smiled! I saw it! Tomorrow! I’ll be at the Alkali Theater. Three o’clock!

speaker: FLARRY
text: Oh! Your ceiling is dripping again. Bernie GRUMBLES.

speaker: BERNIE
text: More leaks? Ember hurries over and gets on the table to melts the pipe shut.

speaker: EMBER
text: Don’t worry. This whole problem is going away. I can feel it. She then sees the CLOCK, it’s 3:00. She hops off the table.

speaker: EMBER
text: And since we’re all good, I am also going away... to do deliveries! Bernie smiles as Ember rushes out. She passes Cinder on the way. INSTANTLY Cinder starts sniffing the air.

speaker: CINDER
text: Do I smell something on... EMBER?? She smiles HUGE.

speaker: CLOD
text: Yo Ember! She jumps, startled, busted.

speaker: EMBER
text: Ah! Clod!

speaker: CLOD
text: I grew another one... He lifts his arm and, with great effort, a tiny flower pops out.

speaker: CLOD
text: Ow! He picks the bud and offers it to Ember.

speaker: CLOD
text: My queen.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oops, sorry! But, gotta go. She hurries off. BEGIN MONTAGE that spans several days:

speaker: CINDER
text: Who IS this guy??

speaker: WADE
text: Whoa. Pan down to reveal she’s standing on a crystal.

speaker: WADE
text: How’d you do that? She picks up a crystal.

speaker: EMBER
text: It’s the minerals. Check this out. Showing off, she twirls onto another crystal and her flame turns PINK.

speaker: WADE
text: Awesome! Ember runs along the shore. Hopping between crystals, her fire changing into all the colors of the rainbow.

speaker: WADE
text: Wow. Smitten and wanting to share, Wade glances toward the water.

speaker: WADE
text: Watch this! He runs onto the surface of the lake and then skids spraying a fine mist into the air, creating a rainbow. He comes to a stop, standing in the water. She looks at him, aglow.

speaker: FIRISH CUSTOMER
text: Sòbê sh sfá.

speaker: EMBER
text: Another?

speaker: FIRISH CUSTOMER
text: Ìshkshá. Ember reaches for a bottle on the top shelf but suddenly there’s a RUMBLE and the shop shakes. An exposed pipe bursts. Water shoots out toward the Blue Flame. Bernie and Ember spring into action.

speaker: EMBER
text: No no no no no no!

speaker: BERNIE
text: The water is back?! They hurry to move the Blue Flame. Bernie starts to cough. Ember runs to melt the pipe together when we hear from O.S...

speaker: DELIVERY PERSON
text: Ember Lumen? Delivery for Ember Lumen? A DELIVERY PERSON enters with SIX VASES of flowers. And ine one of the vases Ember sees TWO EYES! Oh no! It’s Wade! Cinder is so giddy some guy has sent Ember flowers, she doesn’t notice the eyes.

speaker: CINDER
text: Flowers for Ember? Ember quickly finishes repairing the pipe. She runs over to collect the flowers before anyone notices.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh excuse me. Hehe, these are beautiful. I’m gonna go put these away. Arms full, she kicks open the basement door and darts into the basement...

speaker: EMBER
text: What are you doing here? As Wade explains, he pours the water from the other vases into his head. With each pour he grows taller and reforms.

speaker: WADE
text: I got bad news. The sand bags didn’t hold.

speaker: EMBER
text: Uh, obviously!

speaker: WADE
text: Yeah. And I also got worse news. Almost back to form, he pulls his shirt into place and plucks flowers out of his head.

speaker: WADE
text: I’d forgotten a TINY detail about the last time I saw that city crew.

speaker: EX-BOSS
text: You knocked over three tons of cement dust. Half the guys still haven’t recovered... REVEAL a group of angry WATER CONSTRUCTION WORKERS covered in cement, frozen like statues. All they can do is blink.

speaker: WADE
text: ...I guess you could say they still have HARD feelings. Heh heh. Because they won’t help us.

speaker: EMBER
text: Wade, Gale’s deadline is tomorrow. We need more sandbags.

speaker: WADE
text: But that didn’t work before.

speaker: EMBER
text: Well I can’t just do NOTHING!! JUST THEN the basement door opens. It’s Bernie, coming down the stairs.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ember, did you fix leak? Bernie sees Wade.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Huh? It’s you again!!

speaker: WADE
text: Who, me?

speaker: BERNIE
text: You’re guy who started all this! Bernie grabs a poker and swings at Wade.

speaker: WADE
text: Ahhh!

speaker: EMBER
text: No, dad! Different guy. Not all water looks alike.

speaker: BERNIE
text: You are city inspector?

speaker: WADE
text: UhhEMBER No! Right??

speaker: WADE
text: Right. I am not an inspector. Wade puts his hand over his badge, but his water MAGNIFIES the word “INSPECTOR.” He tries again putting his other hand on top but it just magnifies the word even bigger.

speaker: BERNIE
text: You ARE inspector. Why you poking around? Is this because of water leak?

speaker: EMBER
text: No! Not because of water in ANY way.

speaker: EMBER
text: He’s a different kind of inspector. Right??

speaker: WADE
text: Yeah, yeah... I’m a... I'm a... FOOD inspector. I’ve come to inspect your food.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Hmmm. I think he’s lying through his feet.

speaker: EMBER
text: Teeth.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Whatever! Food upstairs. Come. Bernie walks out. Ember shoots Wade a look.

speaker: EMBER
text: Food inspector??

speaker: WADE
text: I panicked!

speaker: BERNIE
text: You really food inspector?

speaker: WADE
text: As far as you know, yeah. Bernie SLAMS a plate of burning kol-nuts on the counter in front of Wade. Wade gulps.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Then inspect THIS.

speaker: EMBER
text: Dad...

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ê... shútsh. Wade leans over the plate and quickly sniffs.

speaker: WADE
text: Yep, all looks good to me.

speaker: BERNIE
text: No no no no. Inspect with your mouth. Bernie takes a big bite and blows fire. Wade, terrified, scoops a kol-nut into a spoon and reluctantly brings it to his mouth. He blows on it, then takes his first flaming bite-- it sizzles all the way down his throat. Flarry, Flarrietta and other customers watch, holding their breath. The coal reaches his belly. Suddenly, Wade screams in pain. He quickly clamps his hands over his mouth to cover. But a giant bubble grows in his body and rises to his head and floats out of his mouth. The bubble floats across the shop and hits Bernie in the head. It POPS:

speaker: WADE
text: AHHHHHHHHHHH! Ember tries to cover.

speaker: EMBER
text: You see? He likes it. Wade coughs, miserable, but smiles and gives a thumbs up.

speaker: BERNIE
text: You must try these. Straight from Fire Land. Bernie pours a pile of coals onto Wade’s plate. Ember realizes what her dad is up to.

speaker: EMBER
text: Dad. Those are too hot.

speaker: WADE
text: I’m okay. I love hot food. Wade’s in too deep. He takes another bite of flaming coals. His head quickly fills with huge air bubbles that immediately POP out of his face and force him backwards. He falls into shelf, spilling water all over the shop.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Hey! Watch your water! So, did we pass?

speaker: WADE
text: Mmmhmmm. A-plus. Actually... After the heat dies down, that’s really good. If you don’t mind... Wade grabs a mug and scoops up more kol-nuts. He then drips water on top of them with his finger. The coals go out with a

speaker: EMBER
text: Mmmm mmmmm! Bernie watches, getting increasingly annoyed. Wade drinks.

speaker: WADE
text: See, it’s really tasty if you water it down a little-- Bad choice of words, Bernie EXPLODES.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Water us down? Water us down?!! Where’s camera?! Bernie pulls out a camera and snaps a photo of Wade’s terrified face.

speaker: BERNIE
text: We will NEVER be watered down by you. Get out!! Ember hurries out from behind the counter.

speaker: EMBER
text: Alright, sir. You gotta go. Wade sets the mug down and rushes for the exit. Bernie huffs and swears in Firish.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ìsh ì k’áì pó?!

speaker: EMBER
text: Dad, don’t worry. I’ve got this.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Khshkff fì íkht’ shíbù áù! Ember runs out after Wade.

speaker: EMBER
text: Look, meet me at the beach and we’ll make more sandbags. We have to figure out how to fix those doors.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Water want to water us down? Then water no longer allowed in shop! He slams Wade’s photo onto the wall under a sign, “BANNED.”

speaker: BERNIE
text: He is panned!

speaker: EMBER
text: Um... banned.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Banned! Agitated, he starts coughing dark smoke. It’s the WORST COUGHING FIT we’ve seen from him to date. Ember rushes to comfort him.

speaker: EMBER
text: Àshfá, it’s okay. It’s all going to be okay. Ember, not so sure it’s going to be okay, looks at Wade’s photo, concerned.

speaker: WADE
text: I don’t think this is going to work.

speaker: EMBER
text: Well it won’t unless you hold the bag straight.

speaker: WADE
text: Maybe your dad will understand. Ember SCOFFS and keeps shoveling.

speaker: WADE
text: I’m serious. Look, I know it can be tough. I mean with my dad... we were like oil and water. I never got a chance to fix that. But you guys are different. It might be time to tell him.

speaker: EMBER
text: Yeah, right. And tell him what?? That I got us shut down and DESTROYED HIS DREAM?? On “dream” she FLARES PURPLE, melts the shovel and lets out a yell. Then collapses to her knees, distraught, her shields down. Her fire slows down and dims to a candlelight, soft, gentle. It’s like we’re seeing her INNER light... the same light from when she was vulnerable in front of city hall. Caustics reflect around her.

speaker: EMBER
text: I think I’m failing. My Àshfá should have retired YEARS ago but he doesn’t think I’m ready. You have no idea how hard they’ve worked or what they’ve had to endure, the family they left behind... How do you repay a sacrifice that big? It all feels like a burden... How can I say that? She hangs her head.

speaker: EMBER
text: I’m a bad daughter.

speaker: WADE
text: Hey, no. You’re doing your best. She shakes this off, not ready to go deeper, then gets uncomfortable with the fragility she is showing Wade.

speaker: EMBER
text: I’m a mess. She tries to collect herself and blaze up to cover her vulnerable light, but before she can:

speaker: WADE
text: Nah, I think you’re even more beautiful. Ember smiles back at him, her flame growing bright again. She looks out at the ocean, the sun meets the horizon.

speaker: EMBER
text: Maybe you’re right when you said my temper is me trying to tell me something. Wade notices where Ember is sitting, the sand is molten.

speaker: WADE
text: Whoa, look what your fire did to the sand. It’s glass. Ember breaks off a piece of the glass, melting it in her hands. She forms it into a sphere and uses her fire to create

speaker: WADE
text: It looks like a Vivisteria flower. SUDDENLY Ember has a realization.

speaker: EMBER
text: I know how to seal those doors. She hops up and runs off, leaving the glass Vivisteria in the sand.

speaker: EMBER
text: Yes! When she finishes, she and Wade stand back and admire her work. Ember is out of breath but her light is peaceful. Wade bites his lip trying hard not to cry.

speaker: EMBER
text: Are you crying?

speaker: WADE
text: Yes. I’ve just never been punched in the face with beauty before. They share a moment. Suddenly the earth VIBRATES. An enormous boat passes through the canal, sending sheets of water into the culvert. They look to the glass as water RUSHES in... they’re poised to run, and take a couple steps back. But the glass holds. It SPLASHES, then settles, only filling the very bottom of the culvert behind the doors.

speaker: EMBER
text: It worked!

speaker: WADE
text: I’ll have Gale come by right after work. I’ll let you know the second I hear anything.

speaker: EMBER
text: You think this'll be good enough for her?

speaker: WADE
text: Honestly? It’s hard to know. She could go either way. Ember nods, nervous. Wade takes something out of his pocket.

speaker: WADE
text: Oh. Here... I saved this for you. He hands her the glass Vivisteria. Ember takes it in her hand.

speaker: WADE
text: It’s special.

speaker: EMBER
text: Àshfá, you okay? He sees her and smiles.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Yes. Yes. Just too much to fix. Ember pulls over a metal can and sits.

speaker: EMBER
text: I’ll take care of it. YOU need to rest. And that’s an order. Bernie salutes.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Yes, ma’am. They share a laugh. Then Bernie sits and takes his daughter’s hands in his own. This is a big moment for him.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ember, I see a change in you. Happier. Calmer with customers, and with that... ...food inspector. He gestures to PHOTO of Wade on the “BANNED!” board.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Always putting shop first. You prove I can trust you. They stand and Bernie heads for the stairs. He stops to cough, then turns back to Ember.

speaker: BERNIE
text: I’m so lucky I have you. He goes upstairs. Ember smiles lovingly. All she wants is to please him. Then her face falls. She pulls out the “going out of business” flyer from Fern, then turns to the picture of

speaker: CINDER
text: Love!

speaker: WADE
text: Ember! You found it! Everything ok?

speaker: EMBER
text: Please tell me that you have some good news from Gale.

speaker: EMBER
text: I'm getting really worried about my dad. This has to break my way.

speaker: WADE
text: Yeah, I haven’t heard from her yet, but she SWORE she’d call tonight. Actually, my family stopped by for dinner? You, want to come up and wait for the call together? Ember pauses. Meet the family? That feels like a new step...

speaker: EMBER
text: Your family? AROUND THE CORNER, unseen by Wade and Ember, Cinder sniffs, still tracking Ember’s trail. She suddenly stops in her tracks. She sees Ember talking to

speaker: EMBER
text: Um... okay, I’ll come up for a bit. Cinder ducks, peering over a ledge.

speaker: EMBER
text: I’m sorry... You LIVE here?

speaker: WADE
text: It’s my mom’s place.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh my gosh. Wade and Ember enter. Hot on their tail, Cinder hurries up to the building and tries to enter. The Doorman blocks her path.

speaker: DOORMAN
text: I’m afraid I can’t let you in. Residents and guests only. Cinder pretends to leave, then suddenly does a spin dodge to get around the doorman, but he quickly extends him arm creating a wall of water.

speaker: CINDER
text: Ah, okay. I under-STAND. Cinder spins again in the other direction, but the Doorman creates a second wall of water with his other arm.

speaker: CINDER
text: You’re surprisingly good at your job!

speaker: DOORMAN
text: You’re surprisingly fast for your age.

speaker: CINDER
text: You have NO idea. Cinder suddenly spins her fire into a tornado! The Doorman gulps, concerned.

speaker: BROOK
text: Ember! Oh I’m so excited to finally meet you.

speaker: BROOK
text: Do we hug or... wave or... don’t want to put you out ha ha.

speaker: EMBER
text: Um, a hello is fine.

speaker: BROOK
text: Hardly. Wade hasn’t stopped talking about you since the day you met. The boy is smitten!

speaker: WADE
text: Mom!

speaker: BROOK
text: Oh, come on. I’m your mother. I know when something’s “lighting you up.” I just didn’t know she’d be so SMOKEY! They all laugh. Ember stops sooner... not as funny to her. A beat.

speaker: BROOK
text: Come this way. Meet the rest of the family. Ember starts to follow but is stopped in her tracks when she sees the apartment is one big swimming pool with floating furniture. Brook steps into the pool and Ember hangs behind. It’s an awkward moment. But, thinking fast, Wade grabs a floaty chair from the side of the room, and then fetches a welcome mat from the foyer. He places the mat on the chair. Solution! Ember cautiously steps onto the floaty. Wade carefully guides Ember into the living room, when Brook remembers:

speaker: BROOK
text: Oh honey! You won’t believe what your baby niece did today! She... she smiled. Brook starts to tear up which gets Wade going.

speaker: WADE
text: No, she didn’t. She nods. They both burst into tears. Ember watches, wideeyed. What has she gotten herself into??

speaker: WADE
text: Hey everyone! This is Ember!

speaker: ALAN
text: Hey!

speaker: WADE
text: That’s my brother Alan and his wife Eddy.

speaker: EDDY
text: Hi!

speaker: ALAN
text: And we got two kids that are swimming around here somewhere. Marco! Polo! Two heads pops out of the water. One chases the other, slapping him with a pool noodle.

speaker: POLO
text: Hi Uncle Wade! Suddenly they see Ember. Whoa. They’ve never been this close to Fire.

speaker: MARCO
text: Do you die if you fall in water? Marco starts to shake Ember’s floaty.

speaker: EMBER
text: Whoa! Wade is furious.

speaker: WADE
text: MARCO! Alan jumps in.

speaker: ALAN
text: Kids. Heh he. Don’t hate us. Alan pulls Marco off and ushers the kids away. Ember regains her balance and smiles uncomfortably. Wade, feeling bad, leads Ember and her floaty over to the dinner table.

speaker: WADE
text: Anyway... that’s my little sib Lake. And her girlfriend Ghibli.

speaker: GHIBLI
text: ‘Sup.

speaker: WADE
text: They’re students at Element City School For the Arts.

speaker: LAKE
text: Yeah, following in Mom’s wake.

speaker: BROOK
text: Oh nonsense, I’m just an architect. The real artist is my brother Harold. Reveal HAROLD, standing in front of a very large painting.

speaker: HAROLD
text: Oh, I just dabble in water colors. Or as we like to call them, “colors.” He laughs at his own joke. Brook sets a dish down on the table and sits. As they talk, everyone takes a seat and the meal begins.

speaker: BROOK
text: Oh don’t listen to him. He’s a wonderful painter. One of his paintings just got in the Element City Museum’s permanent collection.

speaker: EMBER
text: Wow. That is so cool. My only talent is “clean up on aisle 4!”

speaker: WADE
text: Talk about being modest-- Ember’s got an incredible creative flame! I’ve never seen anything like it. Wade looks lovingly toward Ember. Harold jumps in.

speaker: HAROLD
text: I just have to say that you speak SO well and clear-- Wade shoots him a look. Ember keeps her cool.

speaker: EMBER
text: Hmph. Yeah, it’s amazing what talking in the same language your entire life can do.

speaker: HAROLD
text: Doh! Alan tries to break the tension.

speaker: ALAN
text: Uh, hey Ember, did Wadey here ever tell you that he’s deathly afraid of sponges?

speaker: EMBER
text: No.

speaker: WADE
text: I was traumatized... A five-year-old Wade is walking down the school hallway when a janitor drops a large sponge. Curious Wade hangs back and tentatively pokes it. Wade reaches out and touches it with his finger-- it immediately starts to suck in his water. Panicking, he tries to pull away but the suction is too strong and it soaks him up entirely! Close on the sponge with little Wade’s terrified eyes peering out.

speaker: BROOK
text: I still can’t use a sponge around him!

speaker: WADE
text: I was stuck in there for hours! As he laughs, Alan pours from a glass pitcher, but it slips out of his hand and SHATTERS.

speaker: ALAN
text: Oh!

speaker: BROOK
text: Alan! That was new! Ember quickly picks up a couple shards of glass and blows on them, melting them together.

speaker: ALAN
text: My bad. I’m all whirlpools tonight...

speaker: EMBER
text: I can fix it. Without thinking, she gathers all of the pieces and heats them up, melting it into a glowing ball. She then blows into it, shaping it, working it. And we see her light grow vulnerable, prismatic, caustics shining, just like at the dam. She molds the glass into a gorgeous, colorful piece of art. She sets down the pitcher, lost in the moment, and doesn’t notice that the whole family watching mesmerized. She looks up:

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh, uhm... Self-conscious, she ZIPS up her fire.

speaker: EMBER
text: Sorry.

speaker: HAROLD
text: That... was... incredible. The family explodes in applause. Ember tries to cover how flattered she is.

speaker: EMBER
text: It’s just melted glass.

speaker: BROOK
text: Just melted glass? Every building in the new city is built from “just melted glass.” Oh no, you have to do something with that talent. Ember looks to the city through the floor to ceiling windows - - it’s glistening with lights, glass... it’s beautiful. Wade leans to her and whispers.

speaker: WADE
text: See, I told you you’re special. She smiles. It’s a lovely moment. Then Wade suddenly gets an idea! An actual bubble floats up through his head...

speaker: WADE
text: Ooh, thought bubble!

speaker: WADE
text: Maybe after dinner we play “The Crying Game?” Everyone is thrilled with this suggestion. Ember smirks.

speaker: WADE
text: We try NOT to cry.

speaker: WADE
text: You have one minute. Go! Brook and Harold face off against each other.

speaker: HAROLD
text: 1979. November. You were-- Brook bursts into tears. Her team throws their hands up, the other team celebrates. Ember smiles, this is kinda fun.

speaker: BROOK
text: --Never got a chance to say goodbye to Nana. Damn, you’re good.

speaker: HAROLD
text: Okay, Ember, Wade, you’re up. Ember and Wade face off.

speaker: EMBER
text: Yeah this is almost unfair. Because I have literally never cried. You got no chance.

speaker: WADE
text: Sounds like a challenge.

speaker: HAROLD
text: Ready, go!

speaker: WADE
text: Butterfly. Windshield wipers. Half a butterfly. Everyone tears up. Ember is unmoved.

speaker: WADE
text: Okay. An old man on his deathbed. Remembers the summer he fell in love. She was out of his league and he was young and scared. He let her go thinking surely summer would come again. It never did. Ember is still unmoved.

speaker: HAROLD
text: Almost out of time. Wade smiles at Ember, then takes the plunge and be honest. This is no longer about the game, it’s about telling her how he REALLY feels. As he speaks, his family leans in, captivated.

speaker: WADE
text: Ember, when I met you I thought I was drowning. But that light, that light inside you has made me feel so alive. And all I want now is to be near it. Near you. Together. We ZOOM close into Ember’s eye. There is a small glimmering flame in her pupil... it becomes an impressionistic version of EMBER. She floats with WADE and they dance around each other... she sheds her outer light and we see her full vulnerable light as they spin. It’s lovely. They reach out to touch hands, but Ember suddenly pulls back, the magic is broken. She falls away from him... PULL OUT and we see the same reflections on a LAVA TEAR DROP that drips out of Ember’s eye, rolls down her cheek and into the pool. The entire family is caught up in the emotional connection Ember and Wade just made, stunned. Ember and Wade just stare into each other’s eyes. And then, THE PHONE rings and the moment is broken. Wade rushes over to pick it up.

speaker: WADE
text: Hello? Gale, hi. Ember tries to read his expression.

speaker: GALE
text: Glass?? You repaired it with glass?? Behind Gale an Air Worker punches the glass.

speaker: AIR WORKER
text: Hey hey!

speaker: GALE
text: Hold the storm... Wade swallows hard. Ember looks nervous. Gale watches as another worker slams the glass with a large hammer, the glass holds. The workers give her a thumbs up.

speaker: GALE
text: TEMPERED glass? Solid as a rock. I like it. Consider the tickets cancelled. Wade hangs up, smiling, teary eyed with joy. Ember can’t believe it.

speaker: EMBER
text: We did it!?

speaker: WADE
text: Yup! Ember pulls the brochure out her pocket and triumphantly burns it.

speaker: WADE
text: Awesome! Wade runs toward her, arms outstretched.

speaker: WADE
text: Wooo! Ember opens her arms, they’re about to hug but stop themselves.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh! Um.

speaker: WADE
text: Oh. Suddenly, Polo pops up behind Wade and smacks him HARD with a pool noodle.

speaker: WADE
text: Oh!

speaker: EMBER
text: Thank you Mrs. Ripple. This was... this was really great.

speaker: BROOK
text: Yeah, it was. And I mean what I said about your talent. I have a friend who runs the best glass making firm in the world. During dinner, I slipped out and I made a call. And I told her about you. They’re looking for an intern. It could be an amazing opportunity.

speaker: EMBER
text: For real?

speaker: BROOK
text: It’s a long way from the city, but it would be an incredible start. You have a bright future. Look at me! I have an original Ember! Ember’s expression falls. She smiles nervously and turns to go.

speaker: WADE
text: Hold up. I’ll walk you out. Wade follows her out.

speaker: DOORMAN
text: I’m afraid you’re still going to have to wait out here ma’am. Cinder’s tornado slows to a stop. She tries to catch her breath, dizzy.

speaker: CINDER
text: And I'm afraid... I will throw up. Cinder staggers away from the building to recover. Behind her, Wade rushes to catch up to Ember as she hurries to her scooter and hops on.

speaker: WADE
text: Ember! Ember, hold up. What’s going on?

speaker: EMBER
text: I can’t believe she basically offered me a job.

speaker: WADE
text: I know! Could be cool!

speaker: EMBER
text: Yeah, super cool, Wade! I could move out and make glass in a far away city. Do whatever I want.

speaker: WADE
text: I don’t understand.

speaker: EMBER
