text: I’m going home. She starts her scooter. Wade, unsure what else to do:

speaker: WADE
text: Fine, then I’m going with you. Wade hops on the back of the scooter.

speaker: EMBER
text: Ugh! Ember GUNS the scooter and zips into the night. Wade hangs on for dear life. ANGLE ON Cinder, she whips around just in time to see them drive off. She’s SHOCKED:

speaker: CINDER
text: A WATER guy??

speaker: WADE
text: Look-- My mom was just trying to be helpful! She doesn’t know how excited you are to run the shop!

speaker: EMBER
text: Arrgh!! Ember guns it. They weave through traffic. The inertia and GForces stretch Wade out.

speaker: WADE
text: What is the matter??

speaker: EMBER
text: Nothing!

speaker: WADE
text: Yeah? Because we’re going like a thousand, and-- BUS! Ember SWERVES, Wade stretches from the force, splashing along the side of the bus.

speaker: WADE
text: Ah!

speaker: EMBER
text: You don’t know me, Wade! Okay? So stop pretending like you do. They speed toward Firetown.

speaker: WADE
text: What is this about?

speaker: EMBER
text: Nothing. Everything. I don’t know. It’s... She SLAMS the brakes and SKIDS to a stop in front of the shop, the Blue Flame glowing from inside the dark windows.

speaker: EMBER
text: I don’t think I actually DO want to run the shop, okay? THAT’S what my temper has been trying to tell me... I’m trapped. She gets off the bike and faces the shop. Wade joins her.

speaker: EMBER
text: You know what’s crazy? Even when I was a KID, I would pray to the Blue Flame to be good enough to fill my father’s shoes someday. Because this place is his dream. But I never once asked... what I wanted to do. I think that’s because deep down I knew it didn’t matter. Because the only way to repay a sacrifice so big is by sacrificing your life too. Wade stares at Ember.

speaker: CINDER
text: Ember! We see tiny, distant Cinder up on the train platform.

speaker: CINDER
text: Don’t move!!

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh, blaze! My mother. Cinder hurries down the stairs and runs up to them, exhausted from the chase.

speaker: EMBER
text: Mom, it’s okay. He’s just a friend.

speaker: CINDER
text: Si-- SILENCE! I could smell you from over there! You stink.

speaker: EMBER
text: What are you talking about??

speaker: CINDER
text: YOU know what I’m talking about! Ember sniffs herself.

speaker: EMBER
text: You're smelling love on me? Ember glances at Wade... is she... in love?

speaker: CINDER
text: If your father find out. Fire and Water cannot be together! I prove it! Come with me. Cinder goes into the shop, gesturing for them to follow.

speaker: CINDER
text: I splash this on your heart to bring love to the surface! Cinder ANGRILY splashes Ember with oil. Then, she FURIOUSLY splashes Wade, leaving a little oil-slick. He flinches at first but then enjoys it.

speaker: WADE
text: Mmmm. Cinder gestures to the sticks.

speaker: CINDER
text: And then you must light these with your fire and I read the smoke. Ember sniffs herself. Then she lights a stick. Cinder turns to Wade and gestures smugly. His turn. He looks at his finger. No way to light the flame.

speaker: CINDER
text: See, Ember, it cannot be. Wade gets a thought.

speaker: WADE
text: Actually... He stands between Ember and the sticks.

speaker: EMBER
text: What are you doing? He pulls up his shirt and refracts Ember’s light through his torso, causing a magnifying glass effect. He focuses the beam of light onto the stick. And it works! The stick lights and starts smoking, surprising Cinder and Ember both. Everyone watches as the intermingling smoke turns into a double-helix. Cinder can’t help but read the smoke. Ember watches her mom... could it actually tell them something? They are all caught up in the moment when suddenly we hear:

speaker: BERNIE
text: Cinder? Who down there? Ember’s eyes grow in horror.

speaker: EMBER
text: It’s my dad. You have to go!

speaker: WADE
text: Wait, are we a match?

speaker: WADE
text: Ahhh! Bernie approaches from the other side of the shop, unaware.

speaker: BERNIE
text: What's going on? I wake up and nobody upstairs?? After a struggle, we hear Wade escape. Ember tries to cover:

speaker: EMBER
text: It was just me. I was... double checking the locks. And mom came down and...

speaker: CINDER
text: Yes and we... ...began looking at this door. We don’t talk about this door enough!

speaker: EMBER
text: Pull it together! Bernie smiles.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Well, since you are awake... I was going to tell you tomorrow, but... I’m too excited to sleep. In two days... I retire!

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh!

speaker: CINDER
text: Oh Bernie!

speaker: EMBER
text: Two days?

speaker: BERNIE
text: Yes.

speaker: BERNIE
text: We’re going to throw BIG party. A grand reopening! Bernie takes Cinder by the hand and spins her.

speaker: BERNIE
text: That way I can tell whole world my daughter take over. Cinder is overjoyed. Ember looks sick to her stomach.

speaker: BERNIE
text: And I have gift for you. I've had this for awhile, but after our talk I know now is the time. Bernie walks to the counter and pulls out a large box. He puts his hand on it and pauses, getting serious.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Before I give it to you, I need you to understand what it means to me.

speaker: BERNIE
text: When I left Fire Land, I gave my father the “Bà Ksô,” the Big Bow. Bernie turns back toward his parents and does the CEREMONIAL

speaker: BERNIE
text: It is the highest form of respect. But my father did not return the Bow, did not give me his blessing. A heartbroken Bernie, still in the bow, looks to his stoic father.

speaker: BERNIE
text: He say, if we leave Fire Land, we will lose who we are. Bernie hangs his head.

speaker: BERNIE
text: They never got to see all of this. He gestures to the shop, to the shrine.

speaker: BERNIE
text: They didn’t get to see that I NEVER forgot we are Fire. This is burden I still carry. Cinder wells up. Bernie is NEVER this open about his emotions.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ember, it is important that you know you have MY blessing every day you come in here. So I have this made for you.

speaker: EMBER
text: Wow, Àshfá. Bernie flares up, excited, full of YOUTHFUL energy.

speaker: BERNIE
text: It’s gonna be big, bright! Everyone is gonna see this. EMBER’S FIREPLACE! We unveil it at grand reopening!

speaker: CINDER
text: Come Bernie, you need your rest. Cinder leads Bernie away, but turns back and SHOOTS EMBER a look as they go. LATER, Ember sits on the floor, alone in the shop, staring at the sign, turning it on and off. Her flame dims and she quietly starts to cry.

speaker: WADE
text: Ember! So what’d your mom say? About our reading? She’s subdued, holding a box.

speaker: EMBER
text: Nothing. Look, I have a gift for you. Wade steps out into the hall. Ember hands him the box. He reaches in and pulls out the Vivisteria glass.

speaker: WADE
text: And you came all the way here to give it to me? Wait, why are you giving me gifts? Ember looks away. Wade gets it. She’s ending things.

speaker: WADE
text: Oh no. No, no, no, no no. But he gets an idea... bubble, glass, Vivisteria...

speaker: WADE
text: Hold on. I think I have something to show you. Just give me two seconds! And you’re going to need a pair of boots! On Ember, puzzled.

speaker: EMBER
text: Wade, what are we doing here?

speaker: WADE
text: Just wait! They stop in front of a chain link fence. Wade takes off his shirt, throws it over the fence and then walks easily right through, diving back into his shirt on the other side. Ember also walks right through, melting the metal.

speaker: WADE
text: Why do they even have these?

speaker: EMBER
text: Eh, who knows. They continue on toward the station. The NO FIRE sign still stands in the entryway. Wade knocks it over.

speaker: GALE
text: Hey! It’s my favorite fire ball! Ember is puzzled.

speaker: EMBER
text: Hey Gale... what's going on?

speaker: WADE
text: I know you think you have to end this but.. That flooded tunnel? It goes to the main terminal.

speaker: EMBER
text: O-kay?

speaker: WADE
text: Do you still want to see a Vivisteria? Ember is shocked... for real? Wade gestures toward GALE.

speaker: WADE
text: Gale? Gale BLOWS a HUGE bubble in the water, big enough for Ember to get into. She holds the bubble closed at the water’s surface. Ember’s eyes widen as she realizes what Wade’s thinking.

speaker: EMBER
text: Wait, I’m supposed to get in there?

speaker: WADE
text: The air should last...

speaker: GALE
text: ...at least twenty minutes. Ember looks to Wade, hesitant.

speaker: WADE
text: THEY said you couldn’t go in there... Why does ANYONE get to tell you what you can do in your life? Ember looks at the bubble, then back to Wade. He gives her an encouraging nod. She steps to the edge of the water... and jumps in. Inside the bubble, Ember opens her eyes - it worked!

speaker: EMBER
text: Whoa. Getting her bearings, she looks out from the bubble down the long dark passageway. Wade swims around and gives her two thumbs up. He grabs her bubble and starts swimming them deeper. From the surface, Gale waves.

speaker: EMBER
text: A Vivisteria. Ember’s light grows almost entirely prismatic, casting caustics. They’re both in awe. They swim up the vine. As Ember’s light hits the plant, more flowers bloom. Another cloud of fish sends Ember’s bubble spinning.

speaker: EMBER
text: Whoa. More BLOOMS. She glows brighter and the vine EXPLODES with flowers. They swim together through the vines. Ember reaches toward the flowers. It’s a shared, magical moment. BUT suddenly the bubble begins to shrink around Ember as air is used up. The bubble’s wall touches the top of Ember’s flame.

speaker: EMBER
text: Ouch!

speaker: WADE
text: Hey! You’re running out of air! Ember is alarmed. Wade looks around and spots an exit. He swims her shrinking bubble as fast as he can up the stairwell. As Ember’s bubble gets tighter, she starts to hyperventilate, burning up what little oxygen she has left.

speaker: WADE
text: Almost there. Try to breathe slow and steady. Ember nods and manages to slow her breathing down as Wade pushes her up. Wade swims with all his might. They see light-- they must be nearing the surface. They break through just in time.

speaker: WADE
text: I’m so sorry, I should never have-- She springs up onto her feet.

speaker: EMBER
text: Are you kidding? That was amazing. I finally saw a Vivisteria! Wade stands and walks toward her.

speaker: WADE
text: It was inspiring. You were inspiring. Ember looks at him, suddenly serious. Wade just stares into her eyes, calm. He holds his hand out to her.

speaker: EMBER
text: No. Wade, we can’t touch.

speaker: WADE
text: Maybe we can. Ember furrows her brow. She takes a few steps away and stares at the water.

speaker: EMBER
text: No.

speaker: WADE
text: But can’t we just prove it. She turns back to Wade.

speaker: EMBER
text: Prove what?

speaker: WADE
text: Let’s see what happens, and if it’s a disaster then we’ll know this would never work.

speaker: EMBER
text: But it actually could be a disaster. I could vaporize you. You could extinguish me. And then--

speaker: WADE
text: Let’s... let’s start small. Wade holds his hand out to Ember. After a moment, she tentatively reaches towards it. She hovers her hand over his. Her hand flickers and his starts to bubble. She tries again. Placing her palm in his, they TOUCH, in awe. Water boiling, steam, chemistry. They’re touching and they’re okay... equalizing. His water gently simmers from her heat. She pulls her steaming hand away and examines it. She looks to Wade, also inspecting his hand. They step closer and press their fingertips and then their full palms together. Wade’s water pushes back to match the strength of her heat. Their hands find a tingly equilibrium. It’s a magical moment, a coming together like a kiss. They lock eyes then grasp both hands and lean in. In each other’s arms, they dance.

speaker: WADE
text: I’m so lucky. SUDDENLY there’s a quick POP: -- A tired Bernie from before, heading upstairs to bed:

speaker: BERNIE
text: I’m so lucky I have you.

speaker: EMBER
text: I have to go. She runs away along the lake path.

speaker: WADE
text: Wait, what? Where are you going? She hurries up the Wetro stairs and Wade follows.

speaker: EMBER
text: Back to my life at the shop. Where I belong. I take over tomorrow. Wade jumps in front of her, stopping her.

speaker: WADE
text: Whoa whoa whoa, hold up. You don’t WANT that. You said so yourself.

speaker: EMBER
text: It doesn’t matter what I want.

speaker: WADE
text: Of course it does!! She moves past him, up the stairs toward the train platform. Wade, totally confused, follows.

speaker: WADE
text: Listen LISTEN!! You’ve got an opportunity to do something you WANT with your life! This stops her. She turns around.

speaker: EMBER
text: WANT? Yeah, that may work in your rich kid follow-your-heart family. But getting to “do what you want” is a luxury. And not for people like me.

speaker: WADE
text: Why not?? Just tell your father how you feel. This is too important. Maybe he’ll agree.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh, ha. Yeah. There’s no WAY she will do that. Wade sets his jaw.

speaker: WADE
text: Funny. And this whole time I thought you were so strong, but it turns out... you’re just afraid. That hurts... and is probably true. Ember BLAZES.

speaker: EMBER
text: Don’t you DARE judge me. You don’t know what it’s like to have parents who gave up EVERYTHING for you. I’m FIRE, Wade. I can’t be anything more than that. It’s what I am, and what my FAMILY is. It’s our way of LIFE. I cannot throw all of that away JUST FOR YOU.

speaker: WADE
text: I don't understand!

speaker: EMBER
text: And that alone is a reason this could never work. As she boards the train,

speaker: EMBER
text: It’s over, Wade. The train pulls away. Wade is left alone on the platform.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Everyone. Welcome! It is good to see your faces. I am honored to have served you. But it’s time to move on. Come. Ember stands next to him.

speaker: BERNIE
text: My daughter. You are the Ember of our family fire... That is why, I am so proud to have you take over my life’s work. A rope hangs on the microphone stand. Bernie take it in his hands and with a squeeze, he lights it. The rope burns like a fuse leading to decorations covering the sign on the front of the shop. There’s a big BLAZE and when the fire passes we see that Ember’s SIGN is lit up. Everyone applauds.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Pretty good trick, huh? Bernie then gently picks up the lantern and turns to Ember.

speaker: BERNIE
text: This is lantern I bring from old country. Today, I pass it on to you. People in the crowd are touched. Ember reaches to take the lantern, but hesitates. This is a big moment. But just as she’s about to grab it:

speaker: WADE
text: I thought of other reasons. Ember looks up. A Wetro train passes and when the CURTAIN of water clears we see Wade, standing heroic.

speaker: EMBER
text: Wade? Ember is shocked. Cinder looks nervously at Bernie.

speaker: CINDER
text: Oh boy.

speaker: EMBER
text: What are you doing here? Wade walks toward her, entering the party.

speaker: WADE
text: You said me not understanding is the reason we can never work. But I thought of other reasons. A bunch of them. Like, number 1: you’re Fire, I’m Water. I mean, come on that’s crazy. Right? A guest near him nods.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Who is this?

speaker: CINDER
text: No idea. But Wade is focused in on Ember. He continues toward the stage.

speaker: WADE
text: Number 2: I’m crashing your party. Like what kind of a jerk am I? Ember is unflinching.

speaker: EMBER
text: A pretty big one.

speaker: WADE
text: Right? Number 3... I can’t eat your... delicious foods! Wade takes a hot coal from Flarry and Flarrietta’s table. He let’s it boil down his throat. His head bubbles and pops.

speaker: WADE
text: VERY unpleasant.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Wait, I know him! He is food inspector!

speaker: WADE
text: Oh right! Number 4! I’m banned from your father’s shop. He steps closer.

speaker: WADE
text: There are a MILLION reasons why this can’t work. A million “no’s.” But there’s also one “yes.” Ember is being drawn in.

speaker: WADE
text: We touched. Bernie and Cinder are shocked.

speaker: WADE
text: And when we did, something happened to us. Something IMPOSSIBLE. We changed each other’s chemistry. They look deep into one another’s eyes. Suddenly Bernie erupts, exasperated.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Enough! What kind of food inspection is this?? This yanks Ember back to reality.

speaker: WADE
text: A food inspection of the heart, sir. Bernie steps up to Wade, confrontational. Ember is frozen.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Who are you???

speaker: WADE
text: Just a guy who burst into your daughter’s life in a flooded old basement.

speaker: BERNIE
text: So you ARE the one who burst the pipes!

speaker: WADE
text: What? Not me, it was... Wade’s eyes unintentionally dart toward Ember. Ember-- no! Too late. Bernie turns to Ember.

speaker: BERNIE
text: You... you burst the pipe??

speaker: WADE
text: Ember--

speaker: BERNIE
text: Silence!!

speaker: WADE
text: No! Take the chance. Let your father know who you really are. Ember hesitates torn between Wade and Bernie.

speaker: WADE
text: Look, I had regrets when my dad died, but because of you I learned to “embrace the light while it burns.” Tìshók'. You don’t have forever to say what you need to say.

speaker: WADE
text: I love you Ember Lumen. And I’m pretty sure you love me too. Ember is torn between two worlds. She puts up her fire wall.

speaker: EMBER
text: No Wade. I don’t.

speaker: CINDER
text: That’s not true! I did their reading! Everyone is shocked. Including Ember and Wade.

speaker: CINDER
text: Bernie, it’s love. It’s true love.

speaker: EMBER
text: No mom, you’re wrong. Wade, go.

speaker: WADE
text: But, Ember--

speaker: EMBER
text: I DON’T LOVE YOU!

speaker: EMBER
text: GO!

speaker: WADE
text: Wade, defeated, takes the GLASS VIVISTERIA out of his pocket and places it on the stage at Ember’s feet. He turns and walks out, heartbroken. Bernie glares at Ember, devistated.

speaker: BERNIE
text: You have been seeing WATER?? He coughs.

speaker: EMBER
text: Àshfá I--

speaker: BERNIE
text: You caused leak in shop??? I TRUSTED YOU! Ember looks down, unable to confess. Bernie COUGHS more.

speaker: BERNIE
text: You will not take over the shop. I no longer retire. Bernie grabs the lantern. He storms off the stage and into the shop. Ember crushed, looks down at the glass Vivisteria.

speaker: EMBER
text: Why can’t I just be a good daughter? She holds the GLASS Vivisteria ball to her forehead then pulls it away. A look of RAGE and FRUSTRATION crosses her face and she starts to throw it into the water, but she can’t. She looks directly at the glass Vivisteria. We see her reflection WITH a reflection of Element City and Firetown. She is BOTH things. SUDDENLY... she hears a boom in the distance-- CUT TO the CULVERT, as the GLASS from the dam suddenly gives way. A wall of water POURS out. Its enormous force crumbles the sides of the culvert, toppling a towering windmill. BACK WITH EMBER as she sees that all of the PENT UP water RUSHING down the culvert right toward Firetown! A DELUGE is coming and it is heading right toward Bernie’s Shop.

speaker: EMBER
text: Firetown! Ember starts her scooter and rushes off.

speaker: WADE
text: Well, one way ticket to anywhere but here.

speaker: BROOK
text: Go! Travel the world. Heal that broken heart. Brook mimes holding a baby in her arms.

speaker: BROOK
text: My little drip-drip-baby-boy. Tears flow down Wade’s face.

speaker: BROOK
text: Drip drip drip goes the baby boy--

speaker: HAROLD
text: I made you a painting. It’s of a lonely man awash in sadness. Harold hands Wade a painting that is LITERALLY Wade standing and crying, just as he looks right now. Wade bursts into tears. Everyone sobs. SUDDENLY, a siren. Wade instantly stops crying. Through a doorway, he spots steam rising up from Firetown. Oh no.

speaker: WADE
text: Ember.

speaker: EMBER
text: Mom! Dad! But they don’t hear her. Ahead, the river slams into the elevated water train track. The track crashes down, spilling even more water. Determined, Ember speeds up and pulls off the bridge, racing the flood of water toward the shop.

speaker: EMBER
text: Water’s coming!! There’s a panic as Fire folks seek safety. Ember speeds by, the flood at her heels.

speaker: EMBER
text: Watch out! Behind you! Climb! Climb! Flash flood! Hurry! Ember nears the shop and desperately shouts to her parents.

speaker: EMBER
text: Mom! Water! Get to higher ground! Cinder hears and grabs Bernie and pulls him to leave.

speaker: CINDER
text: Bernie! But the deluge catches up to Ember’s scooter and the vehicle starts to hydroplane. Water splashes up on her leg.

speaker: EMBER
text: Ow! The water in the street quickly gets too deep to ride the scooter. Ember leaps off onto a floating truck and looks ahead as water reaches the shop where she sees the flood of water lift up the stage from the party, carrying Bernie and Cinder with it. They cling to each other as they’re tossed about. Bernie lunges toward the shop but Cinder stops him.

speaker: BERNIE
text: The flame! Let me go! Bernie then sees Ember, leaping from floating debris to floating debris, fighting to get to shop.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ember no!! But Ember is on a mission. She gets to the front of the shop and takes one final, giant leap and lands, grasping the transom window over the shop’s front door.

speaker: EMBER
text: Wade?? Wade gestures toward the door handle.

speaker: WADE
text: Key-hole! Getting it, Ember yanks out the key and Wade painfully, squeezes through the keyhole...

speaker: WADE
text: Gahhhhhh!! ...and puddles on the ground. He re-forms and springs into action, joining Ember at the door.

speaker: WADE
text: I was hoping to make a more heroic entrance. Together, face to face, they hold back the door.

speaker: EMBER
text: You came back after everything I said.

speaker: WADE
text: Are you kidding? And miss all this? Ember smiles. Then sees that the water is almost at the BLUE

speaker: EMBER
text: Hold the door! She grabs more sandbags and races to the flame, leaping between shelves. She places sandbags in a circle around the Blue Flame. Then she FLAMES BIG and starts to make glass.

speaker: EMBER
text: No, no. No, no, no, no, no, no, no. And the windows begin to burst from the pressue. The window in the door window behind Wade gives way. There’s no holding it back. The water rises faster. The lantern from Fire Land, the one that carried the Blue Flame to Element City, is swept into the flow. So is the photo of Ember’s childhood birthday.

speaker: WADE
text: Ember we have to go! He strains as water pours in over his shoulders.

speaker: WADE
text: We have to go. NOW.

speaker: EMBER
text: I can’t leave! The water is rising quickly toward the top of her protective glass cylinder.

speaker: WADE
text: I’m sorry to say this but the shop is done. The flame is done.

speaker: EMBER
text: No! This is my father’s whole life. I’m not going anywhere! SUDDENLY, WATER SMASHES through a large window and POURS in, taking everything with it. A shelf topples and SLAMS into Ember’s glass cylinder, SMASHING through the glass and into the flame. A rush of water forces the door open, knocking Wade over. Water and debris surround the Blue Flame. Ember climbs up on a box to avoid the rising water. She shouts to Wade,

speaker: EMBER
text: Throw me that lantern! As Wade swims for the lantern, he’s knocked over by a huge wave.

speaker: WADE
text: Ah! Ember, the flame, and Wade are PUSHED VIOLENTLY toward Cinder’s office in the OLD FIREPLACE HEARTH.

speaker: EMBER
text: No! CUT TO outside the hearth, everything in the shop is underwater. The birthday photo floats by. BACK IN THE HEARTH Ember looks takes in her situation. She’s trapped and the Blue Flame is gone. It appears everything is lost.

speaker: EMBER
text: No, no no no no no. But just then, Wade climbs out from the debris holding a lantern lit with the Blue Flame. Ember is thrilled.

speaker: EMBER
text: Oh Wade!

speaker: EMBER
text: Thank you, thank you. He hands it to her. She smiles, relieved. They share a moment. Then suddenly Ember flinches in pain.

speaker: EMBER
text: Ah! Water has seeped in through the pile of debris, and hit her leg. She looks up at the blocked entrance. Water is squirting through the cracks. She goes to the wall of debris and HEATS it up, flaring hot, melting metal to seal the cracks. And it works! The leaks stop... but Wade is boiling.

speaker: WADE
text: It’s too hot in here. Oh no! They have to get out of there. Ember looks up the chimney stack - it’s open to the sky.

speaker: EMBER
text: Climb! Ember starts to climb and Wade follows...

speaker: EMBER
text: BACK UP BACK UP BACK UP!

speaker: WADE
text: Ah! They drop back down into the hearth. It’s an even tighter space now, more filled with debris. And it’s even hotter. Wade is steaming. Desperate, Ember tries to burn through the wall of debris. Wade reaches up and tries to move the bricks blocking the chimney, but they don’t budge.

speaker: EMBER
text: I have to open that up!

speaker: WADE
text: No! The water will come in and you’ll be snuffed out.

speaker: EMBER
text: But you’re evaporating! I don’t know what to do!

speaker: WADE
text: It’s okay.

speaker: EMBER
text: No, it’s not okay! Wade is boiling. She’s horrified. He takes her hand.

speaker: WADE
text: Ember... I have no regrets. You gave me something people search for their whole lives.

speaker: EMBER
text: But I can’t exist in a world without you! I’m sorry I didn’t say it before. I love you, Wade. Steam surrounds them. Ember’s vulnerable light casts caustics on the wall. Wade looks from her to the caustics around the room with a sense of peace.

speaker: WADE
text: I really do love it when your light does that. They embrace. All is silent. Steam fills the hearth.

speaker: FIRST CUSTOMER
text: They’re in the hearth! A large Fire Element clears debris away from the opening to the hearth. They pull aside the Blue Flame’s base and we reveal: Ember. She’s kneeling on broken bricks, hugging the lantern with the Blue Flame. Relieved, Bernie and Cinder move toward her. But they stop. She’s not getting up or even turning toward them.

speaker: EMBER
text: Wade is gone. Cinder hurries over to her.

speaker: CINDER
text: Oh my daughter.

speaker: EMBER
text: He saved me. Bernie’s face falls, he knows loss. Ember slowly stands, holding the Blue Flame, and she faces Bernie and finally says the most difficult thing she’s ever said:

speaker: EMBER
text: Dad... this is all my fault. The shop... Wade-- I need to tell you the truth. Her flame grows dim, vulnerable.

speaker: EMBER
text: I don’t want to run the shop. I know that was your dream, but it’s not mine. I’m sorry. I’m a bad daughter. Ember hands the lantern to Bernie. He takes it, looks at it in his hand, then puts it down.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ember, the shop was never the dream. YOU were the dream. You were always the dream. Ember looks up, surprised, moved, a burden lifted. She then hugs her dad, crying in his arms. There are no more lies.

speaker: EMBER
text: I loved him dad. Ember kneels, crying. Her light dims even more, cool, loving. Bernie’s matches hers. Cinder joins them. A family, heartbroken, but one unit. Then... we hear a distant, familiar whimper. Ember breaks from her parents’ embrace and looks into the hearth. And that’s when we reveal CONDENSATION in the chimney. A SINGLE DROP of water falls from the ceiling into a bucket. Ember looks up at the condensation... no, it can’t be. She gets an idea and says:

speaker: EMBER
text: Butterfly... She stares hopefully up at the ceiling.

speaker: EMBER
text: Butterfly. Windshield wipers. Half a butterfly. Another whimper. A few more drops fall into the bucket. Ember is energized... maybe it CAN be...

speaker: EMBER
text: An old man on his deathbed remembers the summer he fell in love... More drops. Water starts to pool. Ember gets brighter. We hear more faint weeping from WADE. It’s working, Ember can hardly believe it.

speaker: EMBER
text: She was out of his league and he was young and scared. He let her go thinking surely summer would come again. It never did. Water is falling harder. Wade’s cries are building. Water streams off the ceiling into the bucket. Cinder pieces it together. She looks up at the ceiling...

speaker: CINDER
text: You are perfect match... ten out of ten! More weeping, more rain drops. Bernie looks to Cinder.

speaker: BERNIE
text: I don’t understand. What's going on?

speaker: CINDER
text: Just say something to make Water guy cry, okay? Bernie gives it a try.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Um, uh... you are no longer panned.

speaker: WADE
text: Banned.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Banned. Wade WAILS. Drops POUR. Ember, drops falling all around her,

speaker: EMBER
text: I want to explore the world with you, Wade Ripple! I want to have you with me, in my life. Forever! One more drop and then, they stop. The bucket is full. Ember peers into the bucket... two eyes appear. Then a smile. Wade stands up, alive!

speaker: WADE
text: Whoa. He turns to face them. Naked, he picks up his shirt from the rubble.

speaker: WADE
text: Your, uh, chimney needs cleaning. Everyone is stunned. And then Ember runs to Wade, throwing her arms around him. Then, they KISS! LIGHT shines through the room. It’s breathtaking. Cinder beams. Bernie glances away. Ember and Wade break out to the kiss, look deep into each other’s eyes and embrace.

speaker: CINDER
text: I KNEW it! My nose ALWAYS knows. Everyone laughs.

speaker: CLOD
text: If you were a vegetable, you’d be a cute-cumber. He holds up his arm, revealing a small cluster of blooms. He yanks them out and hands her the bouquet.

speaker: CLOD
text: My queen. We pick up Gale as she walks through, dressed in a Windbreakers jersey.

speaker: GALE
text: Uh! I can’t believe I was gonna shut this place down. She backs up into an Earth Guy who we see is FERN with short, flowering hair. He’s also wearing a Wind Breakers shirt.

speaker: GALE
text: Whoa! Sorry. Fern? You’re a Windbreakers fan?

speaker: FERN
text: Toot toot. Gale is immediately drawn to him.

speaker: GALE
text: Toot. Tooooooot. Flarry and Flarrietta stand behind the counter.

speaker: FLARRIETTA
text: Oy. You know what I like best about running this shop?

speaker: FLARRY
text: Not having to eat Bernie’s kolnuts! Pan to Bernie and Cinder sitting at a table.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Sorry. I couldn’t hear you through my retirement. Everyone BURSTS OUT LAUGHING... the laughing continues as we go outside...

speaker: GALE
text: Ooh! The Fire Girl chases Clod past the counter. Ember watches everyone with fondness. Bernie feeds Cinder a kol-nut. Then, he sees Ember. They share a smile. A connection. But then Wade enters. He yells to the room:

speaker: WADE
text: Hey! Everyone happily turns to the doorway.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Wade!

speaker: CINDER
text: Hi Wade!

speaker: FLARRIETTA
text: Wade!

speaker: FLARRY
text: Hi Wade!

speaker: CLOD
text: Hey Wade! Yo yo yo! Wade turns to Ember...

speaker: WADE
text: Ember, it’s time. Ember smiles, a little sad.

speaker: WADE
text: You know, I’m not really one for tearful goodbyes. Brook bursts out crying.

speaker: BROOK
text: Oh Wade, you big liar. Drip drip drip... Wade joins her in the song. BROOK, WADE Goes the baby boy. They hug, Brook lifting Wade up. They hold each other, uglycrying. Bernie and Ember exchange a look.

speaker: BERNIE
text: Uh, are you sure about this one? Ember smiles.

speaker: EMBER
text: I’m sure. Dad, I’m sorry the internship is so far away. I mean it’s the best glass design company in the world, but who knows if it’ll become a real job. I’ll be back in a few months anyway, and it might not end up being anything--

speaker: BERNIE
text: Ay-yah, shhh. Go. Start new life. Your mother and I will be here. Now with more time for hanky-panky. Cinder gives his side a playful slap.

speaker: CINDER
text: Ê... shútsh! They all laugh. Ember and Wade walk to the boat. Wade starts up the ramp. Ember is about to take a STEP but pauses. She turns to her parents, smiles at Bernie, then sets down her luggage... and then gives the BIG CEREMONIAL BOW. Cinder gasps. Bernie, tears in his eyes, steps forward and
